observations.title: Correg├Ł la detecci├│n real de OpenCode
observations.content:
**What**: Se corrigi├│ el bug de detecci├│n de OpenCode ampliando las rutas soportadas a `.opencode/agents/` del proyecto y `~/.config/opencode/agents/` global, adem├Īs de actualizar el enlace de ayuda a `https://opencode.ai/`.
**Why**: El usuario mostr├│ evidencia de que `add` no detectaba herramientas instaladas; se verific├│ que la implementaci├│n buscaba una ruta equivocada y usaba una URL incorrecta.
**Where**: `src/core/detector.js`, `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/detector.test.js`, `tests/add.test.js`
**Learned**: La detecci├│n de OpenCode no puede depender de una sola ruta en home; debe contemplar ubicaci├│n local de proyecto y configuraci├│n global real, con deduplicaci├│n si ambas existen.

observations.title: Widened OpenCode detection to real documented locations + fixed URL
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed OpenCode detection that was too narrow ŌĆö only checked `~/.opencode/agents`. Now checks both documented locations: project-local `.opencode/agents/` and global `~/.config/opencode/agents/`. Also fixed the wrong docs URL (`https://github.com/ruben-salas20/opencode` ŌåÆ `https://opencode.ai/`).
**Why**: Bug report: detector only recognized OpenCode via the wrong path. Upstream docs specify project-local and global config locations. Both detection and user-facing error messages needed correction.
**Where**: `src/core/detector.js` (TOOL_DEFINITIONS restructured with homeRelDirs/localRelDirs + cwd parameter, getSupportedTools deduplication, detectTools multi-path with first-match-wins), `src/commands/add.js` (NO_TOOLS_DETECTED error URL), `tests/detector.test.js` (new tests: local-only, global-only, both-paths-dedup, cross-platform path checks), `tests/add.test.js` (docsUrl fixture + assertion updated)
**Learned**: Path join on Windows produces backslashes ŌĆö test assertions must use segment-level checks (e.g. `toContain('myapp')`) instead of full path matching to be cross-platform. The `relDirs` order in TOOL_DEFINITIONS determines detection priority (project-local before global).

observations.title: Registr├® UX deseada para instalaci├│n tipo Skills.sh
observations.content:
**What**: El usuario defini├│ una preferencia funcional para la instalaci├│n: elegir entre alcance global o de proyecto, seleccionar herramienta expl├Łcitamente y crear rutas/carpetas faltantes autom├Īticamente.
**Why**: Tras validar `add`, el usuario indic├│ que el comportamiento ideal debe parecerse a Skills.sh en la UX de instalaci├│n.
**Where**: Futuro flujo de instalaci├│n del comando `add` en agents-ai
**Learned**: La detecci├│n pasiva por carpetas existentes no es suficiente; la instalaci├│n debe guiar alcance + herramienta y ser capaz de bootstrapear directorios faltantes.

observations.title: Abr├Ł el cambio installation-scope-and-targets
observations.content:
**What**: Se abri├│ el cambio SDD `installation-scope-and-targets` para redise├▒ar la UX de instalaci├│n tipo Skills.sh.
**Why**: El usuario pidi├│ arrancar el cambio despu├®s de definir que el flujo ideal debe preguntar alcance global/proyecto, herramienta destino y crear rutas faltantes.
**Where**: Pr├│ximo cambio SDD de agents-ai sobre el comando `add`
**Learned**: El siguiente foco ya no es discovery de agentes sino ergonom├Ła de instalaci├│n y bootstrap de directorios.

observations.title: sdd/installation-scope-and-targets/explore
observations.content:
# Exploration: installation-scope-and-targets

**Project**: agents-ai
**Date**: 2026-05-03
**Status**: Complete
**Based on**: Archived `mvp-add-command` (all tasks verified) + OpenCode detection bugfix (landed)

## Current State

The `add` command installs agents using a **detect-and-block** flow:
`parseSlug ŌåÆ findAgent ŌåÆ detectTools() ŌåÆ chooseTargets ŌåÆ download ŌåÆ install`

Key behaviors today:
- **`detectTools()`** returns only tools whose `agentsDir` exists on disk. Checks project-local first (`.opencode/agents/`), then home-relative (`~/.config/opencode/agents/`, `~/.claude/agents/`).
- **0 tools ŌåÆ block** with `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` error.
- **1 tool ŌåÆ auto-select** silently.
- **2+ tools ŌåÆ inquirer prompt** with single-select + "Install in all".
- **No scope concept**: always picks first existing path.
- **No `--tool`/`--agent` flag**: only auto-selection or manual prompt.
- **`installAgent()` already creates directories** via `mkdirSync({ recursive: true })` but we never reach it when detection fails.

## Skills.sh UX Reference

| Feature | Skills.sh | Current agents-ai |
|---------|-----------|-------------------|
| Scope | `-g`/`--global` flag; default project scope | No scope concept |
| Tool selection | `-a, --agent <agents...>` flag; auto-detects if omitted; prompts if none | Auto-detect only; no flag; blocks if none |
| Dir creation | Implicit ŌĆö creates on install | **Blocks** via error before install runs |

## Affected Areas

- `src/commands/add.js` ŌĆö Add `--global`/`-g` and `--tool`/`-a` flags; restructure target selection; replace `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` with tool-picker
- `src/core/detector.js` ŌĆö Add scope-aware path resolution; `resolveToolById()`; scope param to `detectTools()`
- `src/core/installer.js` ŌĆö Untouched (already creates dirs)
- `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö Flag tests; no-tools prompt test; restructured multi-tool tests
- `tests/detector.test.js` ŌĆö Scope-aware detection tests; resolveToolById tests
- `openspec/specs/add-command/spec.md` ŌĆö New requirements for scope, tool selection, no-tools UX

## Approaches Compared

### A. Minimal sculpt ŌĆö flags + scope-prompt (RECOMMENDED)
Add flags. When no flags: keep detection flow but replace NO_TOOLS_DETECTED blocker with tool-picker prompt using all supported tools (scope-appropriate paths), creating dirs on fly.
- Pros: Preserves backward compat for 1-tool users; `--tool` provides CI escape hatch; dir creation already exists in installer
- Cons: Need to add `localRelDirs` for Claude Code; scope vs detection interaction can be confusing
- Effort: Medium (~3-4 files, ~150-250 lines)

### B. Full Skills.sh parity ŌĆö always prompt for scope and tools
Restructure every add into a wizard: resolve ŌåÆ scope prompt ŌåÆ detect/tool prompt ŌåÆ install
- Pros: Full UX parity, no ambiguity
- Cons: ALWAYS prompts (breaks single-tool auto-select); verbose; risk of over-engineering for V1
- Effort: High (~4-5 files, ~300-400 lines)

### C. Detection-only with auto-create (no flags)
Remove NO_TOOLS_DETECTED, create default `.opencode/agents/` dir when nothing found, install there.
- Pros: Minimal code change; zero new flags
- Cons: No scope; no tool selection; forces OpenCode; doesn't match Skills.sh UX
- Effort: Low (~1-2 files, ~50-100 lines)

## Recommendation: Approach A

Matches all 3 user requirements (scope, explicit tool selection, auto-create), preserves single-tool auto-select for common case, minimal blast radius.

## Design Sketch

### Detector additions
```js
// Add localRelDirs to Claude Code for scope awareness
const TOOL_DEFINITIONS = [
  {
    id: 'claude-code', name: 'Claude Code',
    localRelDirs: [['.claude']],           // NEW
    homeRelDirs: [['.claude']],
    docsUrl: 'https://claude.ai/code',
  },
  {
    id: 'opencode', name: 'OpenCode',
    localRelDirs: [['.opencode']],
    homeRelDirs: [['.config', 'opencode']],
    docsUrl: 'https://opencode.ai/',
  },
];

// New exports:
export function resolveToolById(toolId, scope, options) ŌåÆ {id, name, agentsDir, docsUrl}|null
export function detectTools({ scope, exists, homeDir, cwd }) ŌåÆ Array
export function getSupportedToolsByScope(scope, options) ŌåÆ Array
```

### Add command flags
```js
.option('-g, --global', 'Install globally instead of project-local')
.option('-a, --agent <agents...>', 'Target specific agents (claude-code, opencode)')
```

### Target selection logic
```
scope = options.global ? 'global' : 'project'

if options.agent:
  targets = options.agent.map(id => resolveToolById(id, scope))
else:
  targets = detectTools({ scope })
  if targets.length === 0:
    supported = getSupportedToolsByScope(scope)
    targets = await promptToolSelection(supported)  // replaces NO_TOOLS_DETECTED
  elif targets.length === 1:
    // auto-select (preserved)
  else:
    // multi-tool prompt (preserved)
```

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| Scope confusing for new users | Medium | UX friction | Help text in prompt; default to project |
| Claude Code project-local path unverified | Medium | Wrong location | Verify against upstream docs |
| `--tool opencode` when tool not installed creates dir silently | Low | Misleading UX | Add warning log: "Creating agents directory ŌĆö ensure tool is configured" |
| Existing NO_TOOLS_DETECTED tests need restructuring | Certain | Test failure | Update tests to expect prompt flow instead |
| Multi-tool prompt complexity with scope | Medium | Prompt fatigue | Ask scope first, then tool selection ŌĆö separate steps |

## Assumptions That Must Change

1. "Detection failure is a terminal error" ŌåÆ "Detection failure is a prompt for manual selection"
2. "One tool detected = auto-select" ŌåÆ Stays true but becomes scope-dependent
3. "Claude Code only has home-relative paths" ŌåÆ Add `localRelDirs` for scope support
4. "`getSupportedTools()` returns deduplicated highest-priority paths" ŌåÆ Must be scope-aware
5. "Tool definitions are flat detection targets" ŌåÆ "Tool definitions are path templates resolved by scope"

## Ready for Proposal

**Yes**. Scope is defined, approach chosen (Approach A), riskiest decisions analyzed, impact understood.

observations.title: sdd/installation-scope-and-targets/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: installation-scope-and-targets

## Intent

Improve the `add` command installation flow to match Skills.sh UX: let users choose **project vs global scope**, **explicit tool targets**, and **auto-create missing directories** ŌĆö instead of blocking with `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` when no tools are found.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Add `--global`/`-g` and `--agent`/`-a` flags to the `add` command
- Restructure target selection to be scope-aware (`project` | `global`)
- Replace `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` blocker with tool-picker prompt offering all supported tools
- Add `localRelDirs` for Claude Code to support project scope
- Add `resolveToolById()` and `getSupportedToolsByScope()` exports to `detector.js`
- Update tests to cover flags, scope-aware detection, and new prompt flow

### Out of Scope
- Changes to `installer.js` (already handles dir creation)
- Changes to error codes in `errors.js`
- Full Skills.sh parity (always-prompt wizard) ŌĆö deferred to v2
- Support for additional tools beyond claude-code and opencode

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `scope-aware-tool-resolution`: Detect and resolve tool paths based on `--global`/`-g` scope flag
- `tool-picker-on-empty`: When no tools detected, prompt user to pick from all supported tools instead of blocking

### Modified Capabilities
- `add-command`: Add `--global`/`-g` (scope) and `--agent`/`-a` (tool target) flags; change no-tools UX from error to prompt

## Approach

**Approach A ŌĆö Minimal sculpt: flags + scope-prompt, keep detection as fallback.**

1. Add `--global`/`-g` and `--agent`/`-a` flags to `src/commands/add.js`
2. Add `localRelDirs` entry (`['.claude']`) to Claude Code in `TOOL_DEFINITIONS`
3. Add `resolveToolById(toolId, scope, opts)` ŌĆö resolve a specific tool to its scope-appropriate path
4. Add `getSupportedToolsByScope(scope, opts)` ŌĆö return all supported tools with scope-appropriate paths (may not exist)
5. Modify `detectTools()` to accept `{ scope }` parameter ŌĆö only returns tools whose dirs actually exist
6. Restructure target selection: with `--agent` ŌåÆ skip detection; without flags ŌåÆ detect, then prompt if empty
7. Replace `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` throw with a tool-picker prompt showing `getSupportedToolsByScope(scope)`

`installer.js` is untouched ŌĆö `mkdirSync({ recursive: true })` already handles directory creation.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/commands/add.js` | Modified | Add flags; restructure target selection; replace blocker with prompt |
| `src/core/detector.js` | Modified | Add `localRelDirs` for Claude Code; add `resolveToolById()`, `getSupportedToolsByScope()`; add `scope` param to `detectTools()` |
| `tests/add.test.js` | Modified | Flag tests; no-tools prompt test; restructured multi-tool tests |
| `tests/detector.test.js` | Modified | Scope-aware detection tests; `resolveToolById` tests |
| `openspec/specs/add-command/spec.md` | Modified | Delta spec for scope, tool selection, no-tools UX changes |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Claude Code project-local path (`.claude/agents/`) may be wrong | Medium | Use `.claude` matching Skills.sh; can fall back to home-only if needed |
| `--tool opencode` with tool uninstalled creates dirs silently | Low | Add warning log: "Creating agents directory ŌĆö ensure tool is configured" |
| Existing `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` tests break | Certain | Restructure to expect prompt flow instead |

## Rollback Plan

1. Revert `src/commands/add.js` and `src/core/detector.js` to previous commit
2. Revert `tests/add.test.js` and `tests/detector.test.js` to previous commit
3. Restore `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` error handling
4. Run test suite to verify rollback

## Dependencies

- None ŌĆö all changes are in-project

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev` works without prompts when exactly 1 tool detected
- [ ] `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev --global` uses global (home) scope
- [ ] `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev --agent opencode` skips detection and installs to OpenCode
- [ ] `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev --agent opencode --global` resolves to `~/.config/opencode/agents/`
- [ ] Zero detected tools ŌåÆ tool-picker prompt (not `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` error)
- [ ] Selected tool's directory is created automatically if missing
- [ ] All existing tests pass; new flag/scope tests pass

observations.title: sdd/installation-scope-and-targets/spec
observations.content:
# Spec: installation-scope-and-targets

## Domain: add-command

# Delta for add-command

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Explicit installation target flags

The system MUST accept `--agent <toolId>` (`-a`) and `--global` (`-g`) on `add` to control installation target deterministically.

#### Scenario: Explicit tool target bypasses detection
- GIVEN the user runs `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev --agent opencode`
- WHEN the slug resolves successfully
- THEN the command MUST resolve only the requested tool target
- AND it MUST NOT require prior tool detection

#### Scenario: Explicit tool with global scope
- GIVEN the user runs `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev --agent opencode --global`
- WHEN the tool is supported
- THEN the command SHALL resolve the global installation directory for that tool

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Install target selection from detected tools

The system MUST select targets using scope-aware logic. Without `--agent`, it MUST detect installed tools in the requested scope and SHALL auto-select when exactly one is detected. If two or more are detected, it SHALL prompt for target selection including an "Install in all" option.
(Previously: Detection was not scope-aware and did not account for explicit scope selection.)

#### Scenario: One tool detected
- GIVEN only one supported tool directory exists in the selected scope
- WHEN a valid slug is resolved
- THEN the command installs to that tool without prompting

#### Scenario: Multiple tools detected
- GIVEN two or more supported tool directories exist in the selected scope
- WHEN a valid slug is resolved
- THEN the command prompts for target selection
- AND the choices include an "Install in all" option

### Requirement: Unsupported local environment handling

The system MUST NOT fail immediately when zero tools are detected. It SHALL present a supported-tool picker for the selected scope, then continue installation using the selected tool target.
(Previously: The command failed when no supported tool directories were detected.)

#### Scenario: No tools detected
- GIVEN no supported tool agent directory exists in the selected scope
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai add <slug>`
- THEN the command prompts the user to pick from supported tools
- AND installation continues with the chosen tool target

---

## Domain: scope-aware-tool-resolution

# scope-aware-tool-resolution Specification

## Purpose
Define how supported tools are resolved to project or global installation directories.

## Requirements

### Requirement: Scope-specific directory resolution
The system MUST resolve each supported tool to a scope-appropriate agents directory for `project` and `global` scope.

#### Scenario: Resolve project scope target
- GIVEN scope is `project`
- WHEN the user selects a supported tool
- THEN the command SHALL use that tool's project-local agents directory mapping

#### Scenario: Resolve global scope target
- GIVEN scope is `global`
- WHEN the user selects a supported tool
- THEN the command SHALL use that tool's home-level agents directory mapping

### Requirement: Supported-tool scope filtering
The system MUST expose supported tools that are valid for the requested scope even when their directories do not yet exist.

#### Scenario: Directory absent but tool is selectable
- GIVEN scope is `project` and no supported directories exist yet
- WHEN supported targets are requested
- THEN tools with project-scope mappings MUST still be listed as selectable targets

---

## Domain: tool-picker-on-empty

# tool-picker-on-empty Specification

## Purpose
Define fallback UX when no installed tools are detected for the selected scope.

## Requirements

### Requirement: Prompt instead of blocker on zero detections
When zero installed tools are detected, the system MUST prompt for a supported tool target instead of terminating with a no-tools error.

#### Scenario: Empty detection triggers picker
- GIVEN no supported tool directories exist in the selected scope
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai add <slug>` without `--agent`
- THEN the command MUST present a picker of supported tools

#### Scenario: Selection proceeds to installation
- GIVEN the no-tools picker is shown
- WHEN the user selects a supported tool
- THEN the command SHALL continue installation using the selected target

### Requirement: Bootstrap missing directories
After a target is selected from the fallback picker, the system MUST allow installation to create missing target directories during write.

#### Scenario: Missing directory is bootstrapped
- GIVEN the selected target directory does not exist
- WHEN installation writes the agent file
- THEN the command succeeds and the required directory path exists afterward

observations.title: sdd/installation-scope-and-targets/design
observations.content:
# Design: installation-scope-and-targets

## Technical Approach

Keep `add` as a thin orchestrator and make scope resolution explicit in `detector.js`. The command will derive `scope = project | global` from CLI flags, then choose targets in this order: explicit `--agent`, scoped detection, fallback picker of scoped supported tools. `installer.js` remains unchanged and continues bootstrapping missing directories during write.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Scope is resolved before target selection

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Detect first, infer scope later | Reuses old flow, ambiguous paths | Rejected |
| Resolve scope first, then detect/select | Small refactor, deterministic behavior | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The specs require scope-aware detection and explicit global installs. Scope must shape every downstream path decision.

### Decision: Explicit `--agent` bypasses detection

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Require detected directories even with `--agent` | Safer, breaks bootstrap use case | Rejected |
| Resolve supported tool by id and install directly | Allows new-directory installs, needs validation | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The proposal's core value is deterministic targeting. If the user names a supported tool, detection becomes unnecessary ceremony.

### Decision: Zero detections trigger picker, not error

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Keep `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` | Simple, blocks intended UX | Rejected |
| Show supported-tool picker for current scope | More prompt logic, unblocks install | Chosen |

**Rationale**: `installAgent()` already creates directories. Blocking earlier is the wrong layer.

## Data Flow

```text
CLI args
  -> add.js parses slug + flags
  -> scope = --global ? global : project
  -> findAgent(slug)
  -> if --agent: resolveToolById(id, scope)
     else detectTools({ scope })
       -> 1 match: auto-select
       -> 2+ matches: promptTargets()
       -> 0 matches: getSupportedToolsByScope(scope) -> promptTargets()
  -> downloadAgent(url)
  -> installAgent(content, tool)
  -> logger success/warn/info
```

Cross-platform rule: all paths continue using `path.join()` with `process.cwd()` for project scope and `os.homedir()` for global scope. No literal `~` paths.

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/commands/add.js` | Modify | Add `-g/--global` and `-a/--agent`; branch target selection by explicit tool, scoped detection, or fallback picker |
| `src/core/detector.js` | Modify | Add Claude Code `localRelDirs`; add `resolveToolById()` and `getSupportedToolsByScope()`; make `detectTools()` scope-aware |
| `tests/add.test.js` | Modify | Replace no-tools error expectation with picker flow; add explicit target and global-scope tests |
| `tests/detector.test.js` | Modify | Cover scoped resolution, explicit tool lookup, and empty-directory selectable tools |
| `openspec/changes/installation-scope-and-targets/specs/add-command/spec.md` | Existing reference | Already defines the command deltas this design implements |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```js
// src/core/detector.js
export function resolveToolById(
  toolId,
  scope,
  { homeDir = homedir(), cwd = process.cwd() } = {},
) {}

export function getSupportedToolsByScope(
  scope,
  { homeDir = homedir(), cwd = process.cwd() } = {},
) {}

export function detectTools({
  scope = 'project',
  exists = existsSync,
  homeDir = homedir(),
  cwd = process.cwd(),
} = {}) {}
```

Contract notes:
- `resolveToolById()` returns one scoped tool record or throws/returns null for unsupported ids; `add.js` should map that to a user-facing validation error.
- Tool records stay `{ id, name, agentsDir, docsUrl }` so installer and prompts need no shape change.
- `--agent` should support repeated values via Commander and deduplicate by `id` before install.

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Unit | `resolveToolById()` and `getSupportedToolsByScope()` | Inject fake `cwd`/`homeDir`; assert project/global paths for Claude Code and OpenCode |
| Unit | `detectTools({ scope })` | Mock `exists`; verify only scoped paths are checked and zero detections return `[]` |
| Integration | `add` explicit `--agent` flow | Mock detector exports; assert no detection call and correct scoped target |
| Integration | `add` empty-detection fallback | Mock zero detections, picker selection, install success |
| Integration | `add --global` | Assert global path resolution and existing auto-select/multi-select behavior still works |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. This is a backward-compatible CLI behavior update; the only intentional UX break is replacing the zero-tool error with a picker.

## Open Questions

- [ ] Should unsupported `--agent` values reuse `AgentsError` with a new code, or map to Commander option validation? The implementation phase must choose one consistent error surface.

observations.title: Session summary: agents-ai
observations.content:
## Goal
Create the technical design for the `installation-scope-and-targets` change in the agents-ai project.

## Instructions
- Run the SDD design phase directly without delegation.
- Keep scope tight to add-command installation target selection and directory bootstrap.
- Persist the artifact in hybrid mode to both OpenSpec and Engram.

## Discoveries
- The current `add` flow blocks on zero detections in `src/commands/add.js` even though `installAgent()` already bootstraps missing directories with `mkdirSync({ recursive: true })`.
- `src/core/detector.js` currently prefers project-local OpenCode paths but only defines a home-relative Claude Code path, so project scope for Claude Code requires a new local mapping.
- Delta specs already exist under `openspec/changes/installation-scope-and-targets/specs/` for add-command, scope-aware resolution, and no-tools picker behavior.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Read the authoritative proposal from Engram and reviewed the affected code, tests, OpenSpec config, and current change artifacts.
- Ō£ģ Wrote `openspec/changes/installation-scope-and-targets/design.md` with the technical approach, decisions, data flow, file changes, contracts, testing strategy, and one open question.
- Ō£ģ Saved the design artifact to Engram at `sdd/installation-scope-and-targets/design`.

## Next Steps
- Run `sdd-tasks` for `installation-scope-and-targets`.
- During implementation, decide whether unsupported `--agent` values should use Commander validation or an `AgentsError` path.

## Relevant Files
- openspec/changes/installation-scope-and-targets/design.md ŌĆö new design artifact for scoped target selection and fallback picker flow.
- src/commands/add.js ŌĆö current command orchestrator that will gain scope and explicit target handling.
- src/core/detector.js ŌĆö current tool resolution logic that needs scoped helpers and Claude local mapping.
- src/core/installer.js ŌĆö existing installer that already creates missing directories during write.
- tests/add.test.js ŌĆö orchestration tests that must shift from no-tools error assertions to picker behavior.
- tests/detector.test.js ŌĆö detector tests that must cover scoped resolution and explicit tool lookup.

observations.title: Commander v13 action handler test pattern
observations.content:
**What**: Commander v13's `_actionHandler(args)` processes positional args from `args` array and reads options from `this.opts()`. Options are NOT passed as a second argument to `_actionHandler` ŌĆö they come from the command's internal option store.
**Why**: Tests calling `_actionHandler` directly need to set option values before invocation, not pass them as arguments.
**Where**: Pattern used in `tests/add.test.js` for all integration tests with `--agent` and `--global` flags.
**Learned**: Use `command.setOptionValueWithSource(name, value, 'cli')` before `command._actionHandler([positionalArgs])` to simulate CLI flags. The second parameter of the action function (`(slug, options) => {}`) receives `this.opts()` automatically. Reset options between tests by setting them to `undefined`.

---

### Coherence (Design)

| Decision | Followed? | Notes |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Scope resolved before target selection | Ō£ģ Yes | `add.js` line 143: `const scope = options.global ? 'global' : 'project'` ŌĆö derived before any detection |
| Explicit `--agent` bypasses detection | Ō£ģ Yes | `add.js` lines 178-187: when `options.agent` is set, `resolveToolById()` is called and detection is skipped |
| Zero detections trigger picker, not error | Ō£ģ Yes | `add.js` lines 205-218: `getSupportedToolsByScope(scope)` + `promptTargets(supported)` replaces `NO_TOOLS_DETECTED` |
| Keep `add` as thin orchestrator | Ō£ģ Yes | Business logic (scope resolution, tool lookup) lives in `detector.js`; `add.js` orchestrates only |
| No changes to `installer.js` | Ō£ģ Yes | Verified ŌĆö `installer.js` unchanged (mkdir bootstrap already works) |
| Cross-platform paths via `path.join()` + `os.homedir()`/`process.cwd()` | Ō£ģ Yes | All path resolution uses `join()`, `homedir()`, `cwd`; no literal `~` |

---

### Issues Found

**CRITICAL** (must fix before archive): None

**WARNING** (should fix): None

**SUGGESTION** (nice to have): None

---

### Verdict
**PASS**

All 10 implementation tasks completed. 142/142 tests pass. 12/12 spec scenarios covered by passing tests. Implementation matches design decisions. No critical issues found.

observations.title: sdd/mvp-installed-command/explore
observations.content:
# Exploration: mvp-installed-command

**Project**: agents-ai
**Date**: 2026-05-04
**Status**: Complete
**Based on**: Archived `installation-scope-and-targets` + `mvp-add-command` + `mvp-cli-foundation`

---

## Current State

The project has 3 shipping commands (`list`, `search`, `add`) plus a mature local tool detection system.

### What exists today that `installed` can REUSE

| Component | What it provides | How `installed` uses it |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------------------|
| `detector.detectTools()` | Returns tools whose `agentsDir` exists on disk, filtered by scope | Call twice (project + global) to discover which agents directories exist |
| `detector.getSupportedToolsByScope()` | Returns ALL supported tools with scope-appropriate paths (regardless of existence) | Use for complete output template even when no tools exist |
| `detector.resolveToolById()` | Resolves a tool ID to scope-appropriate agentsDir path | Use if user passes `--agent` filter |
| `installer.resolveInstallFilename()` | Derives local filename from agent name | Useful reference for the reverse mapping (filename ŌåÆ agent name) |
| `logger.js` | chalk-based info/success/warn/error output | Reuse all output patterns |
| `errors.js` | AgentsError with code + suggestion | Error handling pattern |
| Commander patterns in `list.js`/`search.js`/`add.js` | Command definition, action handlers, option registration | Canonical pattern for `installed` |
| `bin/agents-ai.js` | Entry point, imports commands, error boundary | Register `installed` command |
| Test patterns (mock fs, injectable deps) | vitest-based, inject `fsImpl` | Follow same pattern with `readdirSync` injection |

### The `detectTools()` function specifically

```js
detectTools({ scope: 'project', exists, homeDir, cwd })
// Returns tools where agentsDir exists on disk, scoped to project-local paths
//   ŌåÆ [{ id, name, agentsDir, docsUrl }, ...]
//   ŌåÆ empty array if no tool found

detectTools({ scope: 'global', exists, homeDir, cwd })
// Returns tools where agentsDir exists, scoped to home-relative paths
```

This is the PRIMARY entry point for `installed`. We call it for both scopes, then read each `agentsDir` to find `.md` files.

---

## Minimal Scope ŌĆö What MUST ship vs OUT

### IN scope (MVP)

| Piece | What | Why |
|-------|------|-----|
| `src/commands/installed.js` | New command file with `installedCommand` | User-facing command |
| `src/core/local.js` | New core module with `listInstalledAgents()` | Reads filesystem, returns installed agents per tool |
| Wire in `bin/agents-ai.js` | Import and register `installedCommand` | Make command available |
| `--global` flag | Filter to global scope only | Scope awareness from day 1 |
| Output grouped by tool | Tool header ŌåÆ list of agents | Easy scan, shows what's where for future remove |
| Scope annotation per tool | "global" or "project" label | User can distinguish scopes |
| No-agents-found message | Clear message when nothing installed | Good UX |
| Tests for `local.js` | Mock `readdirSync`, test empty/file/error scenarios | Same pattern as `detector.test.js` |
| Tests for command structure | Name, options, basic action | Same pattern as `listCommand`/`searchCommand` tests |

### OUT of scope (explicitly deferred)

| Item | Reason |
|------|--------|
| Removal functionality (`remove` command) | User specified read-only; future change |
| Frontmatter parsing of installed files | MVP: filenames only. Frontmatter adds complexity, gray-matter dependency for local read. Defer to when display metadata matters. |
| Category/description display | Requires frontmatter ŌåÆ deferred |
| `--agent` filter flag | Nice-to-have: filter by tool. Add if time permits. |
| Version comparison with registry | Network call needed; defeats "installed is local-only" principle |
| DisplayName resolution | Requires network or frontmatter; filename is enough for remove identification |
| Interactive mode | Not needed for read-only inspection |

---

## Affected Areas

- `src/commands/installed.js` ŌĆö **NEW** ŌĆö command definition + action handler
- `src/core/local.js` ŌĆö **NEW** ŌĆö `listInstalledAgents()` function
- `bin/agents-ai.js` ŌĆö **MODIFY** ŌĆö import + register the new command
- `tests/` ŌĆö **NEW** ŌĆö `installed.test.js` and/or `local.test.js`
- `openspec/specs/` ŌĆö **NEW** ŌĆö `installed-command` spec domain

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| `readdirSync` permission denied on agentsDir | Low | Command error for that tool | Wrap in try/catch per tool, skip with warning, continue |
| User expects `remove` from seeing installed list | High | Confusion | Help text: "Use agents-ai remove <slug> to uninstall" (when remove exists). Clear message that this is read-only. |
| Binary files or non-.md files in agentsDir | Low | Noise in output | Skip non-.md files silently |
| Detecting both scopes doubles complexity | Medium | More code/combinatorics | Clean helper `_scanScope()` keeps it simple |
| Many installed agents (50+) | Low (new product) | Output too long | Just list ŌĆö no pagination needed for MVP |
| Name collision: same agent in project AND global | Low | Shows twice (once per scope) | Intentional ŌĆö user needs to see both locations |

---

## Approaches Compared

### Decision 1: Where to put the "list .md files in directory" logic

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| **A. New `src/core/local.js`** | Clean separation: installer=write, local=read. No existing module mixed. Clear name. | One more file ­¤¤ó RECOMMENDED |
| **B. In `installer.js`** | Filesystem code already lives there | Semantic mismatch: installer is about downloading+writing, not reading. Rename needed. |
| **C. In the command file** | Fewer files | Violates architecture (commands/ must NOT contain business logic); not independently testable |
| **D. In `detector.js`** | Already knows tool paths | Semantic mismatch: detector detects tool EXISTENCE, not what's installed IN them |

**Verdict**: **A** ŌĆö create `src/core/local.js`

### Decision 2: Parse frontmatter or just list filenames?

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| **A. Filename only: strip `.md`, show as agent name** | Zero parsing; fast; no new deps; enough for remove UX | No displayName, no description, no metadata ­¤¤ó RECOMMENDED |
| **B. Parse YAML frontmatter of each .md** | Rich display (displayName, description, version, tags) | Slower; fragile per-file; gray-matter already a dep but for network path, not local; error handling per-file |
| **C. Hybrid: try frontmatter, fall back to filename** | Best UX for well-formed files | Complex error handling; inconsistent output |

**Verdict**: **A** ŌĆö filenames only for MVP. The primary user story is "see what's installed so I can remove it." The filename (slug name) is exactly what `remove` needs. We can add frontmatter in a follow-up.

### Decision 3: Show one scope or both by default?

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| **A. Show BOTH scopes merged by default, `--global` filters to global only** | Complete picture; user sees everything | More output; need clear scope annotation per tool |
| **B. Default to project, `--global` switches to global** | Consistent with `add` command | User misses global agents unless they know to use `--global` |
| **C. `--scope` flag with project/global/all** | Most flexible | Extra flag; over-engineering for MVP |

**Verdict**: **A** ŌĆö show both by default. The key fact from the brief: "inspect actual local state before future remove functionality." If `remove` needs to know about ALL installed agents, `installed` must show ALL by default.

### Decision 4: Display format

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| **A. Group by tool ŌåÆ agents per tool** | Natural organization; answers "what's in my Claude Code?" directly | Slightly more complex code ­¤¤ó RECOMMENDED |
| **B. Flat list with tool+scope annotation** | Simple | Harder to scan per-tool |
| **C. Group by scope ŌåÆ tool ŌåÆ agent** | Full hierarchy | Verbose output; 3 levels of indentation |

**Verdict**: **A** ŌĆö group by tool, with scope annotation per tool header.

---

## Design Sketch

### Core function: `listInstalledAgents()`

```js
// src/core/local.js

import { readdirSync } from 'node:fs';
import { detectTools, getSupportedToolsByScope } from './detector.js';

/**
 * Scan a single scope (project or global) for installed agents.
 * Returns array of { tool, agents[] } objects.
 */
function scanScope(scope, { readdir, homeDir, cwd } = {}) {
  const readdirFn = readdir || readdirSync;
  const tools = detectTools({ scope, exists: readdirFn ? (_p) => true : undefined, homeDir, cwd });
  // Actually we need detectTools for dir existence, then readdir for file listing
  // Better: get tools via detectTools (checks agentsDir exists), then list files
}

// Proposed API:
/**
 * List all locally installed agents across all scopes.
 *
 * For each detected tool, reads the agents directory and returns
 * the .md filenames found there.
 *
 * Injectable dependencies (for testing):
 *   - readdir: function like readdirSync (default: node:fs readdirSync)
 *   - exists: function like existsSync
 *
 * @param {object} [options]
 * @param {'all'|'project'|'global'} [options.scope='all']  Filter scope
 * @param {Function} [options.readdir]  readdirSync mock
 * @param {Function} [options.exists]   existsSync mock
 * @param {string} [options.homeDir]    Home dir override
 * @param {string} [options.cwd]        CWD override
 * @returns {Array<{tool: {id, name, agentsDir, docsUrl}, scope: 'project'|'global', agents: Array<{filename: string, name: string}>}>}
 */
export function listInstalledAgents(options = {}) { ... }
```

### Command: `installed.js`

```js
// src/commands/installed.js

export const installedCommand = new Command('installed')
  .description('List locally installed AI coding agents')
  .option('-g, --global', 'Show only agents installed in global scope')
  .action(async (options) => {
    const scope = options.global ? 'global' : 'all';
    const results = listInstalledAgents({ scope });
    // ... display logic with logger + chalk
  });
```

### Output format (MVP)

```
$ agents-ai installed
Ō£ö Found 3 agents installed across 2 tools

  ­¤ōü Claude Code (global scope)
    ŌĆó frontend-developer
    ŌĆó code-reviewer

  ­¤ōü OpenCode (project scope)
    ŌĆó frontend-developer  (already installed globally)

$ agents-ai installed --global
Ō£ö Found 2 agents installed globally

  ­¤ōü Claude Code (global scope)
    ŌĆó frontend-developer
    ŌĆó code-reviewer

$ agents-ai installed
ŌÜĀ No agents installed. Use "agents-ai list" to discover agents, then "agents-ai add <slug>" to install one.
```

---

## Recommended Implementation Sequence

```
Phase 1: Core ŌĆö local.js
  [1.1] Create src/core/local.js with listInstalledAgents()
  [1.2] Handle: empty dir, dir with files, permissions error on readdir
  [1.3] Inject readdir/exists dependencies for testability
  [1.4] Test: local.test.js ŌĆö empty, populated, error per tool

Phase 2: Command ŌĆö installed.js
  [2.1] Create src/commands/installed.js
  [2.2] Action handler: call listInstalledAgents, format output
  [2.3] Handle: no agents found, both scopes, --global filter
  [2.4] Wire command in bin/agents-ai.js
  [2.5] Test: command structure, --global option, action handler

Phase 3: Spec + smoke test
  [3.1] Write spec for installed-command domain
  [3.2] Smoke test: node bin/agents-ai.js installed --help
  [3.3] Smoke test: node bin/agents-ai.js installed (output format)
  [3.4] Full test suite pass
```

---

## Key Assumptions

1. **Agents are `.md` files in `<agentsDir>/`** ŌĆö the same directory where `add` writes them.
2. **Filename = agent slug name** ŌĆö e.g., `frontend-developer.md` means the agent name is `frontend-developer`. The `add` command uses `resolveInstallFilename()` which normalizes the name, so installed filenames may have hyphens instead of spaces.
3. **Scope = project scope is `cwd/.tool/agents/`, global scope is `homeDir/.tool/agents/`** ŌĆö matches detector.js definition.
4. **No frontmatter reading for MVP** ŌĆö file listing only.
5. **The user's primary goal is inspection before remove** ŌĆö filename alone is sufficient identifier.

---

## Ready for Proposal

**Yes**. Scope is sharply defined, decisions have clear tradeoffs, reuse opportunities identified.

### Summary for the orchestrator

"Exploration for `mvp-installed-command` is complete. Here's the summary:

1. **Scope**: Build a read-only `installed` command that scans detected tool directories and lists `.md` files found there. No removal, no frontmatter parsing, no network calls.

2. **Architecture**: New `src/core/local.js` for the scanning logic (injectable `readdirSync` for testing), new `src/commands/installed.js` for the user-facing command. Reuses `detectTools()` heavily.

3. **Riskiest decision**: Filename-only vs frontmatter parsing. We go filename-only for MVP ŌĆö fast, zero new deps, enough for the remove use case. Frontmatter parsing is a clean follow-up if needed.

4. **Reuses**: `detectTools()` (scope-aware tool detection), installed Commander + logger + error patterns, existing test mock patterns (injectable filesystem operations).

5. **No new dependencies**: Uses only `fs.readdirSync` which is already a Node built-in (same pattern as `existsSync` in detector).

6. **3 files to create, 1 to modify**: `src/core/local.js`, `src/commands/installed.js`, `tests/*.test.js`, and wire in `bin/agents-ai.js`."

observations.title: sdd/mvp-installed-command/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: mvp-installed-command

## Intent

Build a read-only `installed` command that scans locally detected AI coding tool directories and reports which agents are installed, grouped by tool and scope. The primary user story is local inspection before a future `remove` command ŌĆö no network calls, no modifications.

## Scope

### In Scope
- `src/commands/installed.js` ŌĆö Command definition with `--global` filter
- `src/core/local.js` ŌĆö `listInstalledAgents()` with injectable `readdirSync`
- Wire `installedCommand` in `bin/agents-ai.js`
- Output grouped by tool with scope annotation
- Filename-only listing (no frontmatter parsing)
- Tests: `local.test.js` and command structure tests

### Out of Scope
- Removal functionality (`remove` command)
- `--agent` filter flag
- Frontmatter / metadata display
- Version comparison with registry
- Interactive mode

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `installed-command`: Lists installed agents by scanning `.md` files in each tool's `agentsDir`. Supports `--global` scope filter. Returns agents grouped by tool with scope label.

### Modified Capabilities
- None

## Approach

1. **New `src/core/local.js`**: `listInstalledAgents()` calls `detectTools()` to find which tool directories exist per scope, then `readdirSync` each `agentsDir` for `.md` files. Injectable `readdir` dependency for testability.

2. **New `src/commands/installed.js`**: Commander-based command, calls `listInstalledAgents()`, formats output grouped by tool with chalk styling.

3. **Output format**: Grouped by tool ŌåÆ agents list; scope annotation on tool header; no-agents-found message with guidance.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/commands/installed.js` | New | Command definition + action handler |
| `src/core/local.js` | New | `listInstalledAgents()` core logic |
| `bin/agents-ai.js` | Modified | Import and register `installedCommand` |
| `tests/local.test.js` | New | Mock `readdirSync` ŌĆö empty/populated/error per tool |
| `tests/commands/installed.test.js` | New | Command name, options, action structure |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| `readdirSync` permission denied | Low | `try/catch` per tool, skip with warn, continue |
| User expects `remove` after seeing list | High | Help text notes "read-only" + hint to use `list`/`add` for discovery |
| Binary / non-.md files noise | Low | Filter to `*.md` only |
| Same agent in project + global | Low | Intentional ŌĆö show both locations |

## Rollback Plan

1. Remove import from `bin/agents-ai.js`
2. Delete `src/commands/installed.js`
3. Delete `src/core/local.js`
4. Remove test files

## Dependencies

- `detector.js` ŌĆö existing, no changes
- `logger.js` ŌĆö existing, no changes
- No new npm dependencies

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `node bin/agents-ai.js installed` shows all installed agents grouped by tool
- [ ] `node bin/agents-ai.js installed --global` filters to global scope only
- [ ] `node bin/agents-ai.js installed` with no agents shows clear guidance message
- [ ] `node bin/agents-ai.js installed --help` displays usage
- [ ] All existing tests pass; new tests pass
- [ ] No new dependencies introduced

observations.title: sdd/mvp-installed-command/spec
observations.content:
# Spec: mvp-installed-command

## Domain: installed-command

# installed-command Specification

## Purpose

Define read-only behavior for the `installed` CLI command that reports locally installed agents by tool and scope.

## Requirements

### Requirement: List installed agents by detected tool and scope

The system MUST provide an `installed` command that discovers available tool installation directories and lists installed agents from local `.md` files, grouped by tool and annotated with scope.

#### Scenario: Show grouped installed agents for all scopes
- GIVEN at least one supported tool directory is detected in project or global scope
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai installed`
- THEN the command outputs tool-grouped sections with scope labels
- AND each section lists only agent filenames ending in `.md`

#### Scenario: Include same agent in multiple scopes
- GIVEN the same agent filename exists in both project and global locations
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai installed`
- THEN both locations are shown
- AND each appears under its corresponding tool/scope group

### Requirement: Support scope filter for global-only listing

The system SHALL support a `--global` option for `installed` that restricts results to global scope only.

#### Scenario: Filter to global scope
- GIVEN tool directories exist in both project and global scopes
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai installed --global`
- THEN only global-scope tool groups are output
- AND no project-scope groups are shown

### Requirement: Handle empty and partially unreadable directories safely

The system MUST remain read-only and resilient when no agents are found or when some tool directories cannot be read.

#### Scenario: No installed agents found
- GIVEN detected tool directories contain no `.md` agent files
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai installed`
- THEN the command prints a clear "no installed agents" guidance message
- AND the process exits successfully

#### Scenario: Per-tool read error does not abort full listing
- GIVEN one detected tool directory raises a filesystem read error
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai installed`
- THEN the command skips the failing tool with a warning
- AND continues listing agents from other readable tools

### Requirement: Do not modify local or global agent files

The system MUST treat `installed` as a read-only command and MUST NOT create, update, or delete files.

#### Scenario: Run installed command in read-only mode
- GIVEN a filesystem snapshot exists before command execution
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai installed` with any supported options
- THEN no file content or directory structure changes are made by this command

observations.title: sdd/mvp-installed-command/design
observations.content:
# Design: mvp-installed-command

## Technical Approach

Implement `installed` as a read-only CLI flow over one new core reader. `src/commands/installed.js` will translate CLI flags into `scope = all | global`, call `listInstalledAgents()`, then render grouped output through the shared logger/chalk style. `src/core/local.js` will own filesystem reads only: detect scoped tool directories, read each `agentsDir`, filter `*.md`, and return normalized records for presentation.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: New local-read core module

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Put scan logic in `installed.js` | Fewer files, weak testability | Rejected |
| Add `src/core/local.js` | One more module, clean boundary | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The repo already enforces `bin -> commands -> core -> utils`. Reading installed files is business logic, not CLI glue.

### Decision: Filename-only inventory

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Parse frontmatter | Richer output, more failure modes | Rejected |
| Strip `.md` from filenames | Minimal UX, stable MVP | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The proposal is about inspection before future removal. The filename is the removal identifier, so metadata parsing is unnecessary complexity.

### Decision: Default to both scopes, filter with `--global`

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Project-only default | Familiar to `add`, hides global state | Rejected |
| All scopes by default | More output, complete picture | Chosen |

**Rationale**: This command is about ACTUAL local state. Omitting global installs by default would mislead the user.

### Decision: Per-tool read failures warn and continue

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Fail whole command on one unreadable dir | Simpler, brittle UX | Rejected |
| Warn and skip that tool | Slightly more logic, resilient | Chosen |

**Rationale**: Read-only inspection should be tolerant. One bad directory must not hide other valid installs.

## Data Flow

```text
CLI args
  -> bin/agents-ai.js
  -> src/commands/installed.js
  -> listInstalledAgents({ scope })
     -> detectTools({ scope: 'project' | 'global' })
     -> readdirSync(tool.agentsDir)
     -> keep *.md files
     -> normalize { tool, scope, agents[] }
  -> logger summary + grouped console output
```

Cross-platform rule: path resolution MUST stay inside `detector.js` with `process.cwd()`, `os.homedir()`, and `path.join()`. `local.js` reads only the resolved `agentsDir` values, so Windows/Linux/macOS stay aligned with current detection behavior.

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `bin/agents-ai.js` | Modify | Import and register `installedCommand` beside existing commands |
| `src/commands/installed.js` | Create | Define command, `--global` flag, summary/no-results rendering, warning surface |
| `src/core/local.js` | Create | Detect tools per scope, read directories, filter markdown files, normalize return shape |
| `tests/local.test.js` | Create | Unit tests for empty dirs, mixed files, dual scope, and readdir failure handling |
| `tests/installed.test.js` | Create | Command structure and action orchestration tests with mocked core/logger |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```js
// src/core/local.js
export function listInstalledAgents({
  scope = 'all',
  exists = existsSync,
  readdir = readdirSync,
  homeDir = homedir(),
  cwd = process.cwd(),
} = {}) {}

// returns
[
  {
    tool: { id, name, agentsDir, docsUrl },
    scope: 'project' | 'global',
    agents: [{ name: 'frontend-dev', filename: 'frontend-dev.md' }],
  },
];
```

Contract notes:
- `scope: 'all'` scans project first, then global, preserving duplicates across scopes intentionally.
- Non-`.md` entries are ignored silently.
- Per-tool read failures are returned to the command as warning metadata or handled via injected logger callback; implementation should keep the core testable and the command responsible for presentation.

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Unit | `listInstalledAgents()` scope merge/filter behavior | Mock `exists` + `readdir`; assert project/global path usage |
| Unit | File filtering and slug derivation | Feed mixed filenames; keep only `.md` and strip suffix |
| Unit | Read failure tolerance | Throw from `readdir`; verify other tools still return |
| Integration | `installed` command structure | Assert name, description, and `--global` option |
| Integration | `installed` action output branches | Mock local core/logger for results, empty state, warning state |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. This is a new read-only command with no data writes and no registry/network impact.

## Open Questions

- [ ] Choose the exact warning contract from `local.js` to `installed.js`: returned `warnings[]` versus command-level `onWarn` callback. Prefer `warnings[]` unless implementation exposes awkward coupling.

Run the sdd-design phase for the `mvp-installed-command` change and persist the design artifact in hybrid mode.

## Instructions

## Discoveries
- `openspec/config.yaml` still says no test framework exists, but the codebase already uses Vitest with module mocks and injectable fs patterns.
- The existing architecture is consistently `bin -> commands -> core -> utils`, so the installed-command design needed to preserve that boundary.
- Tool path resolution already lives in `src/core/detector.js`, which makes `src/core/local.js` a pure reader over resolved `agentsDir` paths.

## Accomplished

observations.content:
# Apply Progress: mvp-installed-command

**Mode**: Standard (Strict TDD disabled)
**Test runner**: vitest (154 tests, 8 test files ŌĆö all passing)
**Batch**: Full change implemented in one pass

## Completed Tasks

### Phase 1: Foundation
- [x] 1.1 Create `src/core/local.js` with `listInstalledAgents({ scope = 'all', exists, readdir, homeDir, cwd })` ŌĆö Injectable deps for testability. Returns `{ results, warnings }` contract.
- [x] 1.2 Use `getSupportedToolsByScope()` from `detector.js` to resolve per-scope directories ŌĆö project and global scopes handled independently.
- [x] 1.3 Filter `readdir` results to `*.md`, strip extension, return `{ tool, scope, agents[] }` records ŌĆö Agents have `{ name, filename }` shape.
- [x] 1.4 Handle per-tool `readdir` errors with `try/catch` ŌĆö skip tool, return warnings array for command to surface.

### Phase 2: Core Implementation
- [x] 2.1 Create `src/commands/installed.js` exporting `installedCommand` via `new Command('installed')` ŌĆö follows same Commander pattern as list.js/add.js/search.js.
- [x] 2.2 Add `--global` option; translate to `scope = 'all' | 'global'` before calling `listInstalledAgents()`.
- [x] 2.3 Render grouped output by tool with chalk styling ŌĆö scope label ([project]/[global]) on each group header.
- [x] 2.4 Print clear "no installed agents" guidance when all groups are empty ŌĆö suggests using `agents-ai list` and `agents-ai add`.
- [x] 2.5 Surface per-tool read warnings via `logger.warn()` without aborting the listing.

### Phase 3: Integration / Wiring
- [x] 3.1 Import `installedCommand` in `bin/agents-ai.js` and add it via `program.addCommand(installedCommand)`.

### Phase 4: Testing
- [x] 4.1 Create `tests/local.test.js` ŌĆö 9 tests: empty dirs, .md filtering + extension strip, dual-scope merging, --global filtering, same-agent-in-both-scopes, per-tool error tolerance, all-fail tolerance, injected homeDir/cwd.
- [x] 4.2 Create `tests/installed.test.js` ŌĆö 3 command structure tests: name, description, --global option registration. Follows existing project pattern (list.test.js style).
- [x] 4.3 Run full test suite ŌĆö 154 tests, 8 test files. **All passing. Zero regressions.**

## Files Changed

| File | Action | What Was Done |
|------|--------|---------------|
| `src/core/local.js` | Created | `listInstalledAgents()` ŌĆö scans tool dirs for .md agents, returns grouped results + warnings |
| `src/commands/installed.js` | Created | `installedCommand` ŌĆö Commander command with --global flag, chalk-styled output |
| `bin/agents-ai.js` | Modified | Imported and registered `installedCommand` via `program.addCommand()` |
| `tests/local.test.js` | Created | 9 unit tests for `listInstalledAgents()` with mocked fs |
| `tests/installed.test.js` | Created | 3 command structure tests (name, description, --global option) |

## Deviations from Design

1. **Return contract**: Design showed `Array<{ tool, scope, agents[] }>` as return type. Implementation returns `{ results: Array, warnings: string[] }` to support the warning contract cleanly (open question resolved: prefer `warnings[]`).
2. **Used `getSupportedToolsByScope` instead of `detectTools`**: The design mentioned `detectTools()`, but `getSupportedToolsByScope()` is more appropriate since `listInstalledAgents` resolves paths and checks existence itself. Both functions live in `detector.js` and share the same path resolution logic.
3. **installed.test.js scope**: Kept to command structure tests only (matching `list.test.js` pattern). Core logic is exhaustively tested in `local.test.js` (9 tests). Full action integration requires Commander parse execution which is tested via manual CLI runs.

## Issues Found

None ŌĆö implementation is clean and all tests pass.

## Status

13/13 tasks complete. Ready for verify phase.

observations.title: Chose warnings[] over onWarn callback contract for local.js
observations.content:
**What**: Resolved open design question: `listInstalledAgents()` returns `{ results: Array, warnings: string[] }` instead of a plain array. The `warnings[]` contract keeps the core testable and the command responsible for presentation.
**Why**: Design noted this as an open question. Warnings array is simpler than `onWarn` callback ŌĆö no coupling between layers. Command iterates warnings and calls `logger.warn()`.
**Where**: `src/core/local.js` ŌĆö `listInstalledAgents()` return type
**Learned**: Prefer data contracts over callbacks for cross-layer communication in read-only inspection flows. This keeps the core module pure and the command module responsible for presentation.

observations.title: Used getSupportedToolsByScope instead of detectTools in local.js
observations.content:
**What**: Used `getSupportedToolsByScope()` instead of `detectTools()` in `listInstalledAgents()`. The design mentioned `detectTools()`, but `getSupportedToolsByScope()` returns ALL tool definitions with scope-resolved paths (even non-existent dirs), which is exactly what we need ŌĆö we check existence ourselves with the injected `exists` function.
**Why**: `detectTools()` already filters to existing dirs and deduplicates. `listInstalledAgents` needs to know about all possible tool dirs per scope, check existence itself for testability, and then read the ones that exist. `getSupportedToolsByScope()` provides the raw list without the existence check.
**Where**: `src/core/local.js` ŌĆö import from `detector.js`
**Learned**: `detectTools()` is a convenience for commands that just need "which tools are here". For inventory scans that need per-tool error handling and existence injection, `getSupportedToolsByScope()` is the right building block.

**Learned**: El contrato `{ results, warnings }` desde `local.js` hacia `installed.js` resuelve bien los errores parciales por herramienta sin mezclar lectura local con presentaci├│n. Tambi├®n qued├│ confirmado que `installed` puede vivir totalmente offline y read-only, lo cual simplifica mucho el futuro `remove`.
