**Why**: Tras corregir ruido y crashes del flujo actual, el usuario confirm├│ que quiere avanzar con una UX interactiva de selecci├│n m├Īs clara.
**Where**: Pr├│ximo cambio SDD de agents-ai
**Learned**: Ya no basta con prompts de texto correctos; el siguiente salto de calidad est├Ī en el selector de scope/herramientas y la interacci├│n visual del flujo `add`.

observations.title: sdd/install-selection-tui/explore
observations.content:
**What**: Exploration artifact for change `install-selection-tui` ŌĆö TUI-like interactive selection UX for the add command's scope and target prompts.

**Why**: User wants the add selection flow to feel more like skills.sh: clearly visible options, interactive multi-select for scope/target tools, and better affordances than raw prompt text.

**Where**: `openspec/changes/install-selection-tui/exploration.md`, `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/add.test.js`

**Learned**:
- inquirer v13 `checkbox` type (multi-select with spacebar) already available ŌĆö no new dependency needed
- chalk is already a dependency and supports box-drawing characters (ŌĢŁŌöĆŌĢ«ŌĢ░ŌöĆŌĢ») for summary rendering
- `_normalizeTargets()` already handles Array returns from checkbox prompts (the `list` type wrapped single selections in arrays, checkbox natively returns arrays)
- Three approaches compared: (1) checkbox + chalk visual polish (recommended), (2) checkbox-only minimal, (3) full TUI library (deferred)
- Risks are low: box chars degrade gracefully, chalk strips in CI, checkbox edge cases handled by existing guards
- No changes to `package.json`, `detector.js`, `installer.js`, or `utils/` needed

observations.title: sdd/install-selection-tui/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: install-selection-tui

## Intent

Improve the interactive target selection UX in `add` to feel more like skills.sh ŌĆö multi-select checkboxes, chalk-colored choice labels, and a box-drawn summary ŌĆö using only existing dependencies (inquirer + chalk).

## Scope

### In Scope
- Switch `promptTargets()` from `type: 'list'` (single-select) to `type: 'checkbox'` (multi-select) in `add.js`
- Add chalk-colored tool labels with `_describeTool()` enriched names + dim paths in checkbox choices
- Add `inquirer.Separator()` + instructional hint ("Space to toggle ŌĆó Enter to confirm") to target prompt
- Replace dashed-line summary with box-drawn layout using chalk/box chars
- Light polish to `promptScope()` with separators and chalk labels
- Update `tests/add.test.js` for checkbox type, choice count, and summary format assertions

### Out of Scope
- No new dependencies (no `@clack/prompts`, `ink`, etc.)
- No broader CLI redesign ŌĆö scope/target/summary only
- No changes to `detector.js`, `installer.js`, `package.json`, or `utils/` (beyond logger if needed)

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `tool-multi-select`: Multi-select checkbox prompt for target tool selection. User toggles tools with spacebar, confirms with Enter. Returns array ŌĆö handled natively by existing `_normalizeTargets()`.

### Modified Capabilities
- `add-command` Requirement: "Install target selection from detected tools" ŌĆö the "Install in all" single shortcut is replaced by native multi-select behavior; no functional requirement change
- `install-summary` Requirement: Pre-install summary rendering ŌĆö visual format changes from dashed lines to box-drawn chars; requirements unchanged

## Approach

**Inquirer `checkbox` + chalk visual polish** ŌĆö same stack, surface UX upgrade only.

1. `promptTargets()`: swap `type: 'list'` ŌåÆ `type: 'checkbox'`; enrich choice labels with chalk (bold cyan tool name, dim path); add `Separator()` + hint footer
2. `renderSummary()`: replace `ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ Installation Summary ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ` dashes with `ŌĢŁŌöĆŌöĆ Installation Summary ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌĢ«` box-drawn layout using chalk.cyan/yellow/green for values
3. `promptScope()`: add `Separator()` between choices; chalk polish on labels (cosmetic)
4. Tests: update prompt type assertions (`list` ŌåÆ `checkbox`), choice count assertions, and summary string assertions

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/commands/add.js` | Modified | `promptTargets()`, `promptScope()`, `renderSummary()` ŌĆö checkbox, chalk labels, box summary |
| `tests/add.test.js` | Modified | Update mock assertions for prompt type, choice structure, summary format |
| `openspec/specs/add-command/spec.md` | Modified | Delta: checkbox replaces "Install in all" shortcut conceptually |
| `openspec/specs/install-summary/spec.md` | Modified | Delta: summary visual format note only |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| `checkbox` returns empty array if Enter pressed with nothing selected | Medium | `_normalizeTargets()` already handles `[]` ŌåÆ triggers "No target" warning + abort |
| Box-drawing chars break on Windows cmd.exe raster fonts | Low | chalk strips gracefully; fallback dashes work everywhere |
| `chalk.level === 0` in CI | Low | chalk auto-strips; readable plain text output still correct |
| Test refactoring introducing incorrect assertions | Medium | Patterns already exist; only type/choice string values change |

## Rollback Plan

Revert `src/commands/add.js` and `tests/add.test.js` to prior versions. No schema, config, or dependency changes. Instant rollback with `git checkout HEAD~1 -- src/commands/add.js tests/add.test.js`.

## Dependencies

- `inquirer@^13.0.0` ŌĆö already present; `checkbox` type is native
- `chalk@^5.0.0` ŌĆö already present; box chars and `chalk.color` support native

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `agents-ai add <slug>` with 2+ detected tools shows checkbox multi-select prompt
- [ ] Summary renders in box-drawn style with chalk colors (degrades to plain text if no color support)
- [ ] `_normalizeTargets()` correctly handles array return from checkbox
- [ ] Existing tests pass after updating type and string assertions
- [ ] No new dependencies added

observations.title: sdd/install-selection-tui/spec
observations.content:
# Spec: install-selection-tui

## Domain: add-command

# Delta for add-command

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Install target selection from detected tools

The system MUST select targets using scope-aware logic. Without `--agent`, it MUST detect installed tools in the requested scope and SHALL auto-select when exactly one is detected. If two or more are detected, it SHALL present a multi-select target prompt that allows selecting one or many tools and returns the selected tool set for installation.

(Previously: Multiple detected tools were handled by a single-choice prompt that included an "Install in all" shortcut option.)

#### Scenario: One tool detected

- GIVEN only one supported tool directory exists in the selected scope
- WHEN a valid slug is resolved
- THEN the command installs to that tool without prompting

#### Scenario: Multiple tools detected

- GIVEN two or more supported tool directories exist in the selected scope
- WHEN a valid slug is resolved
- THEN the command prompts for multi-select target selection
- AND the user MAY select one or many tools before continuing

#### Scenario: No tool selected in multi-select

- GIVEN two or more supported tool directories exist in the selected scope
- WHEN the user confirms the prompt with no selected tools
- THEN the command MUST abort installation without writing files

---

## Domain: install-summary

# Delta for install-summary

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Pre-install summary visibility

Before writing files in interactive mode, the system MUST display a summary of selected slug, resolved scope, selected tools, and resolved install paths. It SHALL annotate paths that do not yet exist as `(will be created)`. The summary SHOULD be rendered as a boxed, structured layout with clear visual grouping while preserving readable plain-text output when color/styling is unavailable.

(Previously: Summary output requirements did not specify structured boxed rendering.)

#### Scenario: Summary shows target details

- GIVEN at least one installation target is resolved
- WHEN interactive installation is about to start
- THEN the command MUST print slug, scope, tools, and target paths

#### Scenario: Missing directory is marked

- GIVEN a resolved target path does not exist yet
- WHEN summary is rendered
- THEN that path entry includes `(will be created)`

#### Scenario: Styled summary degrades to plain text

- GIVEN terminal color or style support is unavailable
- WHEN summary is rendered
- THEN required summary fields remain visible and readable in plain text

observations.title: sdd/install-selection-tui/design
observations.content:
# Design: install-selection-tui

## Technical Approach

Upgrade the existing `add` command UX in place, without changing command flow or dependencies. The implementation stays inside `src/commands/add.js`, reusing `inquirer`, `chalk`, `_describeTool()`, and `_normalizeTargets()` so the new multi-select prompt and boxed summary remain a surface-level enhancement aligned with the proposal and delta specs.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Keep the current command-layer ownership

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Move prompt/summary formatting into `logger.js` or a new presenter | Cleaner separation, but unnecessary file churn for a small UX change | Rejected |
| Update `src/commands/add.js` only | Follows the current command orchestration pattern and keeps scope tight | Chosen |

**Rationale**: `add.js` already owns prompt composition and pre-install summary rendering. Keeping the UX logic there matches the projectŌĆÖs command/core/utils layering.

### Decision: Replace ŌĆ£Install in allŌĆØ with checkbox multi-select

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Keep `list` prompt plus ŌĆ£Install in allŌĆØ shortcut | Preserves current behavior, but cannot express arbitrary subsets | Rejected |
| Use `checkbox` and let users choose any subset | Better UX, native array output, no new dependency | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The delta spec now requires one-or-many selection. `checkbox` returns an array that fits `_normalizeTargets()` and the existing install loop.

### Decision: Render styled text, not a layout engine

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Add dynamic width helpers or a new TUI library | More control, far more complexity | Rejected |
| Build fixed boxed lines with Unicode chars + chalk | Simple, testable, and degrades to plain text when styling is stripped | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The CLI already uses `chalk`, and tests assert strings, not terminal dimensions. A simple boxed layout is enough.

## Data Flow

```text
User runs add
   |
scope resolution
   |
detectTools(scope)
   |
   +-- 1 tool  ------> auto-select
   +-- 2+ tools -----> checkbox prompt -> selected array
   +-- 0 tools ------> supported-tools checkbox prompt -> selected array
                           |
                     _normalizeTargets()
                           |
                     renderSummary()
                           |
                     confirm prompt
                           |
                  downloadAgent -> installAgent(each target)
```

Cross-platform note: Unicode box characters and chalk colors MUST remain readable when styling is stripped, especially on Windows terminals with limited glyph/color support.

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `src/commands/add.js` | Modify | Change `promptTargets()` to `checkbox`, add separators/hints, polish `promptScope()`, and replace dashed summary with boxed output |
| `tests/add.test.js` | Modify | Update prompt assertions, choice expectations, and summary-output assertions for checkbox + boxed summary |
| `openspec/changes/install-selection-tui/design.md` | Create | Technical design for this change |

## Interfaces / Contracts

No public API changes. Internal expectations:

```js
promptTargets(tools, scope) => Promise<Array<Tool>>

choice = {
  name: string,   // chalk-styled label allowed
  value: Tool,
}
```

Implementation detail: each checkbox choice should map to a single `Tool`; ŌĆ£select allŌĆØ is now native user behavior, not a synthetic combined value.

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|---|---|---|
| Unit | `_normalizeTargets()` still handles `[]`, objects, and invalid shapes | Keep existing helper tests; add empty-checkbox path coverage if missing |
| Integration | Scope prompt labels, target prompt type/message, separator/hint presence, multi-select install behavior | Update `tests/add.test.js` mocked `inquirer.prompt` assertions |
| E2E | None in this phase | No separate E2E harness exists; rely on command integration tests |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. Rollout is immediate because the change is limited to interactive `add` UX.

## Open Questions

- [ ] Whether the boxed summary should use fixed-width borders or minimal top/bottom framing when paths are very long.

observations.title: Designed install selection TUI approach
observations.content:
**What**: Chose an in-place UX upgrade for `add` using `inquirer` checkbox prompts and a chalk-styled boxed summary, without introducing new abstractions or dependencies.
**Why**: The change scope is intentionally tight around add-command selection UX, and the existing command-layer patterns already own prompt composition and summary rendering.
**Where**: `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/add.test.js`, `openspec/changes/install-selection-tui/design.md`
**Learned**: The repo already has Vitest configured despite `openspec/config.yaml` still describing testing as unavailable, so design should target existing integration-style command tests rather than inventing new infrastructure.

observations.title: Inquirer mock must include Separator class
observations.content:
**What**: vitest mock of the `inquirer` package must include `Separator` class in addition to `prompt` function when the code under test uses `new inquirer.Separator()`.

**Why**: The test mock overrides the entire default export, hiding `inquirer.Separator` which is normally available on the real package.

**Where**: `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö vi.mock('inquirer', ...) now includes a Separator class mock with `type`, `line`, and `name` properties.

**Learned**: If code uses any inquirer export beyond `prompt` (like `Separator`, `ui`, etc.), the mock must expose them. The `name` property on the fake Separator must be set to the constructor argument so choice iteration works.

observations.title: Chalk-wrapped strings work with expect.stringContaining in vitest
observations.content:
**What**: Chalk-styled choice label strings (e.g., `${chalk.cyan.bold('Claude Code')} ${chalk.dim('(project)')}`) can be tested with `expect.stringContaining('Claude Code')` without issues ŌĆö vitest's ANSI-aware output doesn't interfere with substring matching.

**Why**: Chalk ANSI escape codes wrap the content but don't alter the plain text substring positions.

**Where**: All tests in `tests/add.test.js` that check choice label content.

**Learned**: No need to strip ANSI codes in test assertions for chalk-wrapped strings when using `stringContaining` or `toContain`. The substring is still present between ANSI codes.

observations.title: Archiv├® install-selection-tui
observations.content:
**What**: Se complet├│ y archiv├│ el cambio `install-selection-tui`, reemplazando la selecci├│n simple por un checkbox multi-select para herramientas y mejorando el resumen previo con una caja visual estilizada.
**Why**: El usuario quer├Ła que la selecci├│n del flujo `add` se sintiera m├Īs como skills.sh, con opciones visibles y selecci├│n interactiva m├Īs clara.
**Where**: `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/add.test.js`, specs sincronizados en `openspec/specs/add-command/spec.md` y `openspec/specs/install-summary/spec.md`
**Learned**: El mayor salto de UX vino sin nuevas dependencias: `inquirer` con `checkbox` + `Separator` y un resumen boxed con `chalk` ya alcanzan una experiencia mucho m├Īs cercana a skills.sh. Tambi├®n se confirm├│ que reemplazar ŌĆ£Install in allŌĆØ por multi-select real simplifica el modelo mental y los tests.

observations.title: Scope prompt footer separator with navigation instructions
observations.content:
**What**: Added a footer inquirer.Separator to the `promptScope()` function in `add.js`, mirroring the structure of `promptTargets()`. The scope prompt now has header separator + explicit options + footer separator with interaction instructions ("Arrow keys to navigate ŌĆó Enter to select"), making it feel like a real chooser consistent with the tool-selection step.

**Why**: The scope prompt was missing the footer usage-instruction separator that the tool prompt has. Users reported the scope step felt less selectable/clear than the tool step. Adding the footer separator makes it visually consistent and gives users clear interaction hints.

**Where**: `src/commands/add.js` ŌĆö `promptScope()` function (added footer Separator, updated header text, updated prompt message). `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö updated choice length assertions (3ŌåÆ4), added 2 new tests for footer separator structure and header separator labels.

**Learned**: Inquirer prompts feel incomplete without usage-instruction footers. The pattern is: header separator (what you're choosing) ŌåÆ options ŌåÆ footer separator (how to interact). Both scope (list) and tool (checkbox) prompts should follow this same structure for a consistent UX.

observations.title: Session summary: agents-ai
observations.content:
## Goal
Implemented a focused UX bugfix for the `add` command's scope-selection prompt in `agents-ai` ŌĆö making it visually consistent with the tool-selection prompt by adding a footer separator with navigation instructions.

## Instructions
- No new SDD cycle ŌĆö tightly scoped fix only.
- Keep scope step consistent with tool-selection step structure.
- No new dependencies.
- Preserve current architecture/testability.

## Discoveries
- The scope prompt (`promptScope()`) was missing a footer `inquirer.Separator` with usage instructions ŌĆö the tool prompt (`promptTargets()`) had `ŌöĆŌöĆ Space to toggle ŌĆó Enter to confirm ŌöĆŌöĆ` but scope had nothing.
- The pattern for inquirer prompts should be: header separator ŌåÆ options ŌåÆ footer separator. This makes each step feel like a "real chooser."
- The mock `inquirer.Separator` in tests sets `name` to the separator's `line` value, allowing string assertions on separator content.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Added footer `inquirer.Separator('ŌöĆŌöĆ Arrow keys to navigate ŌĆó Enter to select ŌöĆŌöĆ')` to `promptScope()` in `src/commands/add.js`
- Ō£ģ Updated header separator from `ŌöĆŌöĆ Scope ŌöĆŌöĆ` to `ŌöĆŌöĆ Installation scope ŌöĆŌöĆ` for context clarity
- Ō£ģ Updated prompt message from `Select installation scope:` to `Choose installation scope:`
- Ō£ģ Updated `tests/add.test.js`: choice length assertion 3ŌåÆ4, message text match, added 2 new tests (`scope prompt includes footer separator with navigation instructions`, `scope prompt header separator identifies the section`)
- Ō£ģ All 227 tests pass across 10 test files

## Relevant Files
- `src/commands/add.js` ŌĆö `promptScope()` function (lines 104-125): added footer separator, updated header and message
- `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö Updated scope prompt tests and added 2 new structural tests (lines 1004-1062)

observations.title: Pul├Ł el selector de scope en add
observations.content:
**What**: Se ajust├│ el prompt de selecci├│n de scope en `add` para que siga el mismo patr├│n visual que la selecci├│n de herramientas: encabezado claro, opciones visibles y footer con instrucci├│n de navegaci├│n.
**Why**: El usuario detect├│ una inconsistencia de UX: el alcance de instalaci├│n no se sent├Ła tan seleccionable ni claro como el selector de herramientas.
**Where**: `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/add.test.js`
**Learned**: La consistencia entre prompts importa mucho en CLIs interactivos; repetir el patr├│n header ŌåÆ options ŌåÆ footer de navegaci├│n reduce fricci├│n y hace que el flujo se sienta m├Īs tipo skills.sh incluso sin cambiar de librer├Ła.

observations.title: Changed scope prompt from list to checkbox type matching tool-selector UX
observations.content:
**What**: Changed `promptScope()` in `src/commands/add.js` from `type: 'list'` to `type: 'checkbox'` with a `validate` guard enforcing exactly one selection. This matches the `promptTargets()` interactive family ŌĆö same visual toggle indicators (ŌŚŗ/ŌŚē), same Space-to-select interaction, same visible rendering of all options at once. The `list` type felt like a "text field / hidden collapsed choice"; checkbox is an explicit visible interactive chooser.

**Why**: User reported via screenshot that the scope step still didn't feel like the tool selector. The previous fix (header/footer separators) addressed visual structure but not the core interaction type. The tool selector uses `checkbox` for visible multi-select; scope should use the same type with single-choice enforcement.

**Where**: 
- `src/commands/add.js` ŌĆö `promptScope()` function: changed `type` from `'list'` to `'checkbox'`, removed `ŌŚÅ` dot prefix from labels (checkbox provides its own indicator), added `validate(selected)` that returns error string when `selected.length !== 1`, changed footer from "Arrow keys to navigate ŌĆó Enter to select" to "Space to select ŌĆó Enter to confirm", added defensive empty-array check with `NO_SCOPE_SELECTED` error
- `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö updated `setupPromptSequence` to push `{ scope: [scope] }` (checkbox returns arrays), changed `type` assertion from `'list'` to `'checkbox'`, updated footer separator text assertions, added new test "scope checkbox enforces exactly one selection via validate" that verifies `validate(['project']) === true`, `validate([])` returns error, and `validate(['project', 'global'])` returns error

**Learned**: Inquirer `checkbox` type always returns an array, so `promptScope()` extracts `answer.scope[0]` to maintain the existing string contract (`'project' | 'global'`) for all downstream consumers (detectTools, resolveToolById, getSupportedToolsByScope, renderSummary). The `validate` function receives the current selected array and must return `true` or string error ŌĆö it does NOT prevent the user from toggling multiple options, it only blocks Enter submission until exactly one is selected.

observations.title: Hice visible el selector de scope
observations.content:
**What**: Se cambi├│ el prompt de scope en `add` de `list` a `checkbox`, para que se comporte visualmente como un selector visible igual al de herramientas, con validaci├│n de exactamente una opci├│n elegida.
**Why**: El usuario report├│ con evidencia que el scope segu├Ła sinti├®ndose como un campo/selecci├│n poco visible y quer├Ła que se pareciera al selector interactivo de herramientas.
**Where**: `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/add.test.js`
**Learned**: Para mantener consistencia de UX en un CLI interactivo, incluso una elecci├│n de cardinalidad 1 puede beneficiarse de usar el mismo patr├│n visual de checkbox si eso hace las opciones expl├Łcitas y visibles. La clave es devolver luego un solo valor normalizado y validar exactamente una selecci├│n.

observations.title: Fixed contradictory UX messaging in add command zero-detection fallback
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed contradictory messaging in `add` command when `detectTools()` returns zero tools. Previously the CLI logged "No installed tools detected" then called `promptTargets()` which said "(${tools.length} detected):" ŌĆö creating a confusing UX where it claimed zero detected then said "2 detected."

**Why**: The `promptTargets` function always used the word "detected" regardless of whether tools were actually found on disk or were fallback supported choices. When passing fallback tools (from `getSupportedToolsByScope()`), these are NOT detected ŌĆö they're supported choices whose directories may not exist yet.

**Where**:
- `src/commands/add.js` ŌĆö `promptTargets()` gained optional `{ descriptor }` param (default 'detected', set to 'supported' in fallback). Zero-detection messages changed from "No installed tools detected" to "No existing tool directories were found" (both interactive and -y paths).
- `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö Updated message assertions, added new test verifying fallback prompt uses "supported" not "detected".

**Learned**: The word "detected" implies on-disk existence confirmation. In fallback scenarios where directories don't exist yet, use "supported" or "available" to accurately convey that these are known tools the CLI can install into (creating directories as needed).

observations.title: Correg├Ł mensaje contradictorio de detecci├│n en add
observations.content:
**What**: Se corrigi├│ el mensaje contradictorio del flujo `add` cuando no existen directorios previos: ahora distingue entre herramientas detectadas y herramientas soportadas elegibles para crear rutas nuevas.
**Why**: El usuario mostr├│ que la CLI dec├Ła ŌĆ£No installed tools detectedŌĆØ y acto seguido mostraba ŌĆ£2 detectedŌĆØ, lo cual era enga├▒oso aunque el flujo pudiera continuar creando directorios.
**Where**: `src/commands/add.js`, `tests/add.test.js`
**Learned**: En este flujo el caso de cero detecciones no significa ŌĆ£no puedes instalarŌĆØ, sino ŌĆ£no existen directorios todav├ŁaŌĆØ. La UX correcta debe hablar de *supported choices* y de creaci├│n de rutas, no de ausencia total de herramientas.

observations.title: Gap analysis: agents-ai MVP vs vercel-labs/skills
observations.content:
**What**: Gap analysis completo entre agents-ai (v0.1.0 MVP) y vercel-labs/skills (v1.5.3). agents-ai tiene arquitectura s├│lida (command pattern, DI testing, errores custom) pero soporta solo 2 agentes, 5 comandos, y una fuente de registro. skills soporta 52 agentes, 8 comandos, m├║ltiples fuentes (GitHub, GitLab, SSH, local, well-known), lock files, telemetr├Ła, symlinks, y un ecosistema completo.

**Why**: Identificar brechas para roadmap de pasar de MVP a producto p├║blico.

**Where**: D:\Mis Archivos\Documentos\Proyectos\agents-ai\ vs https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills

**Learned**: agents-ai tiene mejor calidad de testing (DI patterns, errores custom) pero carece de: update, init, find commands; lock files; telemetr├Ła; 50+ agentes; m├║ltiples formatos de source; CI/CD; build step; symlinks; security audit; plugin marketplace; ecosystem website. skills tiene TypeScript, obuild bundler, husky hooks, lint-staged. Decisi├│n cr├Łtica: converger hacia modelo skills o diferenciarse.

observations.title: Identified roadmap gaps against PRD
observations.content:
**What**: Compar├® el proyecto agents-ai con vercel-labs/skills y verifiqu├® adem├Īs brechas contra el PRD del proyecto.
**Why**: El usuario pidi├│ evaluar qu├® falta para pasar del MVP a una versi├│n p├║blica utilizable y revisar alineaci├│n con el PRD.
**Where**: agents-ai-PRD.md, package.json, bin/agents-ai.js, src/core/registry.js, src/core/detector.js, src/commands/add.js.
**Learned**: El c├│digo actual ya tiene buena base arquitect├│nica, pero no cumple puntos clave del PRD como multi-registry real, soporte Cursor V2, README p├║blico, cache local, sugerencias de agentes y consolidaci├│n de fuentes en list/search; adem├Īs la propuesta p├║blica sigue estando por debajo de la paridad m├Łnima con vercel-labs/skills.

observations.title: Implemented multi-registry catalog merge
observations.content:
**What**: Implement├® el primer paso de producto para agents-ai: multi-registry real en el cat├Īlogo, con prioridad para el registry oficial, merge deduplicado por slug y b├║squeda ampliada por displayName y tags.
**Why**: El PRD exige registry oficial + fallback a agency-agents; adem├Īs list/search deb├Łan dejar de depender de una sola fuente para acercar el MVP a una beta p├║blica utilizable.
**Where**: src/core/registry.js, tests/list.test.js, tests/search.test.js.
**Learned**: La mejor regla de precedencia es "primer registry gana" por slug category/name; as├Ł el cat├Īlogo propio puede sobrescribir community/fallback sin romper descubrimiento del resto de agentes.

observations.title: sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/explore
observations.content:
## Exploration: official-registry-bootstrap

### Current State
The CLI agents-ai has a multi-registry cascade defined in src/core/registry.js:
1. RegistrySource (agents-ai) ŌåÆ ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/main/registry.json (404 ŌĆö DOES NOT EXIST)
2. FileTreeSource (msitarzewski/agency-agents) ŌåÆ 147+ agents (the only working source)

The project depends 100% on a third-party repo. The official registry from the PRD has never been created. The code is already wired to use it (DEFAULT_SOURCES in registry.js lines 103-113), but the URL returns 404 so the cascade always falls through.

### Affected Areas
- ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry (new repo) ŌĆö creation of the official registry repo
- src/core/registry.js ŌĆö ZERO changes needed (already points to correct URL, already handles PRD format)
- src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js ŌĆö ZERO changes needed (remains the fallback)
- src/commands/list.js ŌĆö will begin showing agents with source: "agents-ai" once registry exists
- src/commands/add.js ŌĆö findAgent() will find local registry agents first
- tests/list.test.js ŌĆö could add integration test for cascade with mock data

### Findings

1. **Minimal artifacts needed**: registry.json (what RegistrySource.list() fetches) + agent .md files (what getAgentDownloadUrl downloads). README.md is optional for beta but recommended for a public repo.

2. **PRD format vs current code**: The PRD format { version: "...", agents: [...] } is EXACTLY what the code expects as its preferred shape. The code already handles it via _normalize(). The source field from PRD entries is ignored (code overwrites it with this.name). RECOMMENDATION: use PRD format exactly.

3. **Repo structure and min data**: 
   - Repo: agents/{category}/{name}.md + registry.json + README.md
   - Required registry.json fields: name, category, path (path is critical ŌĆö MISSING_PATH error if absent)
   - Optional: description, tags, author, version
   - Minimum for beta: 3-5 agents in 2-3 categories

4. **Risks of markdown agents**: NO technical risk ŌĆö .md files are the standard format for Claude Code/OpenCode agents. The CLI just copies them. Real risks: desync between registry.json and .md files (HIGH), rate limiting on raw.githubusercontent.com (MEDIUM), no end-to-end testing (MEDIUM).

5. **Minimum implementation for next apply**: Create the GitHub repo, registry.json with 4 initial agents, agent .md files, README.md. Verify end-to-end. Add cascade integration tests. ZERO code changes needed in the CLI.

### Recommendation
Create the agents-ai-registry repo as priority #1. The CLI code is ready ŌĆö the only missing piece is the registry itself. Four initial agents proposed: frontend-dev, debugger (engineering), reddit-ninja (marketing), technical-researcher (research).

### Risks
1. Empty registry ŌåÆ CLI falls back to agency-agents (works but defeats the purpose)
2. Path inconsistency ŌåÆ add command fails with 404 on download
3. No cache on RegistrySource ŌåÆ rate limiting under heavy use  
4. Single point of failure if repo is deleted/renamed
5. No format validation ŌåÆ silent failure if registry.json is malformed

### Ready for Proposal
Yes. Full analysis complete. Next step: sdd-propose.

observations.title: Official registry bootstrap ŌĆö zero code changes needed in CLI
observations.content:
**What**: La implementaci├│n del registry oficial (ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry) requiere CERO cambios en el c├│digo del CLI agents-ai. El DEFAULT_SOURCES en src/core/registry.js ya apunta a la URL correcta, el RegistrySource ya maneja el formato { version, agents: [...] } del PRD, y el cascade ya prioriza agents-ai sobre agency-agents.

**Why**: Se investig├│ el cambio SDD 'official-registry-bootstrap' y se descubri├│ que el c├│digo est├Ī completamente preparado ŌĆö solo falta crear el repo del registry.

**Where**: src/core/registry.js (DEFAULT_SOURCES lines 103-113, RegistrySource class, _normalize method)

**Learned**: El PRD y el c├│digo est├Īn alineados en el formato de registry.json. La prioridad #1 es crear el repo GitHub con registry.json + agentes .md. Los agentes iniciales recomendados: frontend-dev, debugger (engineering), reddit-ninja (marketing), technical-researcher (research). Riesgo principal: desincronizaci├│n entre registry.json y los archivos .md en el repo.

observations.title: sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: official-registry-bootstrap

## Intent

Formalize the creation of the official `agents-ai` registry repository to stop the 404 cascade in the CLI's multi-registry lookup. The CLI already has the correct URL wired (`ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry`), but the repo does not exist. This change bootstraps the minimum viable registry so `RegistrySource` becomes functional.

## Scope

### In Scope
- **Repo creation**: Create `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` GitHub repo (public)
- **registry.json**: PRD format `{ version, agents: [{ name, category, path, description, tags, author, version }] }` with 4 initial agents across 2ŌĆō3 categories
- **4 agent markdown files**: `agents/{category}/{name}.md` with YAML frontmatter (name, category, description, author, version) and usage content
- **README.md**: Minimal project documentation for a public registry repo

### Out of Scope
- CLI code changes (registry.js, sources, commands) ŌĆö already correct
- Integration or E2E tests
- Format validation pipeline
- More than 4 initial agents
- registry.json schema validation on push
- Cache layer for RegistrySource (defer to future change)

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `registry-bootstrap`: Single new top-level capability representing the creation of the official registry repo with seed content. Each agent in registry.json becomes a spec entry under this capability.

### Modified Capabilities
- None. Existing capabilities (registry-fetch, file-tree-source, list-command, add-command) are not changing requirements ŌĆö this change only populates the data source they already reference.

## Approach

Create the GitHub repo manually. Structure follows PRD conventions already respected by the CLI:
- Repo: `agents/{category}/{slug}.md` + `registry.json` + `README.md`
- `path` field in registry.json entries is critical ŌĆö must match the actual file path in repo
- 4 initial agents: `engineering/frontend-dev`, `engineering/debugger`, `marketing/reddit-ninja`, `research/technical-researcher`
- Use PRD format exactly (code already handles it via `_normalize()`)

Zero CLI code changes. Verify end-to-end by running `agents-ai list` and checking agents appear with `source: "agents-ai"`.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` | New | Official registry repo ŌĆö creation is the deliverable |
| `src/core/registry.js` | None | Already wired to correct URL ŌĆö no changes |
| `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js` | None | Remains fallback only |
| `src/commands/list.js` | None | Will surface agents with source: "agents-ai" once registry exists |
| `src/commands/add.js` | None | `findAgent()` will find local registry agents first |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Empty registry (repo created but no agents) | Low | Populate 4 agents in same PR; verify list output before merge |
| Path mismatch ŌåÆ add command fails | Medium | Strictly follow `agents/{category}/{name}.md` pattern; verify path in registry.json matches |
| RegistrySource has no cache ŌåÆ rate limit under heavy use | Medium | Accept for beta; add disk cache in future change if needed |
| Repo deleted/renamed ŌåÆ CLI breaks | Low | Document repo name constraint; do not rename without coordination |
| Malformed registry.json ŌåÆ silent failure | Low | Manual review before publish; add JSON schema validation in future |

## Rollback Plan

Delete the `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` GitHub repo. The CLI immediately falls back to `agency-agents` (FileTreeSource) ŌĆö no code change needed. This is the current behavior and is fully functional.

## Dependencies

- GitHub account `ruben-salas20` must have permission to create public repos
- No external services, CI systems, or package dependencies required

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` repo exists and is public
- [ ] `registry.json` is accessible at `main/registry.json` and returns HTTP 200
- [ ] `agents-ai list` shows agents from the new registry with `source: "agents-ai"`
- [ ] `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev` successfully downloads the agent markdown file
- [ ] No CLI code files are modified

observations.title: Chose data-only bootstrap for official registry
observations.content:
**What**: Chose to implement official-registry-bootstrap as a data-only registry publication, keeping the CLI unchanged and relying on the existing RegistrySource + FileTreeSource cascade.
**Why**: The current failure is the missing upstream `agents-ai-registry` repository, not incorrect multi-registry logic in `src/core/registry.js`.
**Where**: openspec/changes/official-registry-bootstrap/design.md, src/core/registry.js, agents-ai-PRD.md
**Learned**: `getAgentDownloadUrl()` depends on `registry.json` paths matching the repository tree exactly and appends `.md` itself, so the registry content must be disciplined to avoid runtime failures.

observations.title: sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/spec
observations.content:
# Spec Artifact: official-registry-bootstrap

## Domain: registry-bootstrap

# registry-bootstrap Specification

## Purpose

Definir el contrato m├Łnimo y verificable del registry oficial `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` para que el CLI use su `RegistrySource` primario sin cambios de c├│digo.

## Evidence

- PRD `agents-ai-PRD.md` (secci├│n 6): estructura del repo y formato de `registry.json`.
- Comportamiento actual del CLI `src/core/registry.js`:
  - `DEFAULT_SOURCES` prioriza `agents-ai-registry` y luego `agency-agents`.
  - `getAgentDownloadUrl()` deriva URL desde `registry.json` + `agent.path` + `.md`.
  - `_normalize()` soporta campos PRD y asigna `source` al nombre del source.

## Requirements

### Requirement: Estructura m├Łnima del repositorio oficial

El repositorio oficial del registry MUST existir como repositorio p├║blico y SHALL incluir `registry.json` en la ra├Łz, `README.md` en la ra├Łz y un ├Īrbol `agents/{category}/{name}.md` para agentes seed.

#### Scenario: Estructura m├Łnima v├Īlida
- GIVEN el repositorio `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry`
- WHEN se inspecciona la rama `main`
- THEN existe `registry.json` en la ra├Łz
- AND existe `README.md` en la ra├Łz
- AND existen archivos `.md` bajo `agents/*/*.md`

### Requirement: Formato de `registry.json`

`registry.json` MUST ser un JSON objeto con `version` (string) y `agents` (array). Cada item de `agents` MUST incluir `name`, `category`, `path`, `description`, `tags`, `author`, `version`, con tipos v├Īlidos y `path` relativo sin prefijo `/`.

#### Scenario: Manifest con shape PRD
- GIVEN un `registry.json` del registry oficial
- WHEN se parsea el documento
- THEN contiene `version` string y `agents` array
- AND cada agente contiene los campos requeridos con tipo correcto

#### Scenario: Path inv├Īlido
- GIVEN un agente cuyo `path` no es relativo o no apunta a `.md`
- WHEN el manifiesto se valida contra este spec
- THEN el manifiesto se considera no conforme

### Requirement: Conjunto m├Łnimo de agentes seed

El registry oficial SHALL publicar al menos estos slugs iniciales en `registry.json`: `engineering/frontend-dev`, `engineering/debugger`, `marketing/reddit-ninja`, `research/technical-researcher`.

#### Scenario: Seeds presentes
- GIVEN el `registry.json` inicial del registry oficial
- WHEN se listan sus slugs `category/name`
- THEN contiene exactamente los 4 slugs seed definidos

### Requirement: Consistencia `registry.json` Ōåö markdown

Para cada entrada en `registry.json`, el archivo en `path` MUST existir y ser Markdown. Para cada archivo seed en `agents/*/*.md`, MUST existir una entrada correspondiente en `registry.json` con el mismo slug. El frontmatter SHOULD mantener coherencia sem├Īntica con `name`, `category`, `description`, `author`, `version`.

#### Scenario: Entrada sin archivo
- GIVEN una entrada en `registry.json`
- WHEN su `path` no existe en el repositorio
- THEN el registry se considera inconsistente

#### Scenario: Archivo seed sin entrada
- GIVEN un archivo seed en `agents/*/*.md`
- WHEN no existe entrada equivalente en `registry.json`
- THEN el registry se considera inconsistente

### Requirement: Comportamiento esperado del CLI con registry oficial disponible

Sin modificar c├│digo del CLI, cuando `agents-ai-registry/main/registry.json` responde 200 y contiene seeds v├Īlidos, `list` MUST mostrar agentes con `source: "agents-ai"`; `add <slug-seed>` MUST resolver el agente en el source oficial y construir URL de descarga desde `path`; si el source oficial falla, el CLI SHALL mantener fallback al source secundario.

#### Scenario: list prioriza source oficial
- GIVEN `registry.json` oficial accesible con seeds v├Īlidos
- WHEN se ejecuta `agents-ai list`
- THEN los agentes seed aparecen con `source: "agents-ai"`

#### Scenario: add descarga desde path oficial
- GIVEN el slug `engineering/frontend-dev` existe en el source oficial
- WHEN se ejecuta `agents-ai add engineering/frontend-dev`
- THEN el CLI deriva la URL desde `registry.json` + `path` + `.md`
- AND la descarga se realiza desde el repo oficial

#### Scenario: fallback cuando source oficial no disponible
- GIVEN el source oficial devuelve error (ej. 404)
- WHEN se ejecuta `agents-ai list` o `agents-ai add` para un slug existente en fallback
- THEN el CLI contin├║a con el source secundario sin crash

observations.title: Verified CLI registry bootstrap assumptions
observations.content:
**What**: Confirm├® que el bootstrap del registry oficial no requiere cambios de c├│digo del CLI y depende de la existencia/consistencia de `registry.json` + `path`.
**Why**: La spec deb├Ła basarse en evidencia del PRD y del comportamiento real del CLI.
**Where**: `agents-ai-PRD.md` (secci├│n 6), `src/core/registry.js` (`DEFAULT_SOURCES`, `_normalize`, `getAgentDownloadUrl`).
**Learned**: El campo `source` del manifiesto no gobierna el origen final en runtime para RegistrySource; el CLI lo normaliza al nombre del source configurado.

observations.title: sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/design
observations.content:
# Design: official-registry-bootstrap

## Technical Approach

Implement the change as a DATA bootstrap, not a CLI refactor. The current CLI already resolves multiple sources through `RegistrySource` and `FileTreeSource`; this change makes the primary source real by creating `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` with the PRD shape expected by `src/core/registry.js`. In this repo, the only artifact change is this design; runtime behavior is validated against existing commands and tests.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Keep the CLI unchanged

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Modify `registry.js` to special-case bootstrap | More code and risk for a repo-creation problem | Rejected |
| Publish the missing registry data source | Depends on disciplined content structure, but preserves proven CLI flow | Chosen |

**Rationale**: The failure is a missing upstream repository, not a broken cascade. TOUCHING the CLI would hide the real problem.

### Decision: Use PRD registry paths exactly

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Store arbitrary file paths and adapt code later | Flexible, but breaks current download derivation | Rejected |
| Use `agents/{category}/{name}.md` in `registry.json.path` | Slightly stricter authoring, zero CLI changes | Chosen |

**Rationale**: `getAgentDownloadUrl()` derives the raw markdown URL by stripping `registry.json` from the source URL and appending `path + .md`.

### Decision: Validate by contract, not by new runtime features

| Option | Tradeoff | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| Add schema validation/cache/features now | Better guardrails, larger scope | Rejected |
| Keep bootstrap minimal and verify existing contracts | Manual discipline, fastest safe delivery | Chosen |

**Rationale**: Proposal scope explicitly excludes new CLI capabilities.

## Data Flow

```text
agents-ai list/add
  -> src/core/registry.js DEFAULT_SOURCES
  -> RegistrySource("agents-ai") fetches registry.json
  -> _normalize() maps entries to agent contract
  -> listAgents()/findAgent() merge with FileTreeSource fallback
  -> getAgentDownloadUrl() => {registry-base}/{path}.md
```

Primary contract for the new repo:
- `registry.json` MUST return `{ version, agents: [] }`
- each agent MUST include `name`, `category`, `description`, `path`, `tags`, `author`, `version`
- each markdown file MUST exist at `agents/{category}/{name}.md`

Cross-platform note: no OS-specific code changes are needed in this repo. Registry paths stay repository-relative POSIX paths, while local install paths remain handled by existing Node path logic.

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `openspec/changes/official-registry-bootstrap/design.md` | Create | Technical design artifact for this change |
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/registry.json` | Create | Official catalog consumed by `RegistrySource` |
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/agents/engineering/frontend-dev.md` | Create | Seed agent 1 |
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/agents/engineering/debugger.md` | Create | Seed agent 2 |
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/agents/marketing/reddit-ninja.md` | Create | Seed agent 3 |
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/agents/research/technical-researcher.md` | Create | Seed agent 4 |
| `github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry/README.md` | Create | Minimal public registry documentation |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "agents": [
    {
      "name": "frontend-dev",
      "category": "engineering",
      "description": "...",
      "path": "agents/engineering/frontend-dev",
      "tags": ["react", "typescript"],
      "author": "ruben-salas20",
      "version": "1.0.0"
    }
  ]
}
```

`RegistrySource._normalize()` will add `source: "agents-ai"` automatically from the configured source name; the repo content SHOULD NOT depend on a `source` field being honored.

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Contract | Registry manifest shape and path consistency | Manual review of `registry.json` against actual repo tree before publish |
| Unit (existing repo, optional) | Primary registry entries normalize and win duplicate merge | Extend `tests/list.test.js` with injected `RegistrySource` payloads only if extra confidence is needed |
| Smoke | Listing and exact lookup | Run `node bin/agents-ai.js list` and `node bin/agents-ai.js add engineering/frontend-dev --yes` after the registry is public |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. Rollout is: create repo, add `registry.json`, add 4 markdown files, publish on `main`, then smoke-test the existing CLI.

## Open Questions

- [ ] Should the registry repo add a lightweight JSON validation workflow immediately, or defer completely to a later hardening change?

## Goal
Crear el dise├▒o t├®cnico SDD para el cambio `official-registry-bootstrap` en `agents-ai`.

## Instructions
- Usar evidencia del c├│digo actual y del PRD.
- Leer la propuesta desde el topic_key `sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/proposal`.
- artifact_store.mode: hybrid.
- Dise├▒ar la implementaci├│n m├Łnima del registry y evitar tocar m├Īs c├│digo del CLI.

## Discoveries
- `src/core/registry.js` ya apunta al registry oficial `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` y resuelve multi-registry con `RegistrySource` primero y `FileTreeSource` como fallback.
- `getAgentDownloadUrl()` deriva la URL final removiendo `registry.json` del source URL y concatenando `path + .md`; por eso el campo `path` del registry debe coincidir EXACTAMENTE con el ├Īrbol real del repo.
- El cambio no necesita c├│digo nuevo del CLI; el problema real es la ausencia del repo upstream, no la l├│gica de cascada.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Le├Ł la propuesta completa desde Engram (`sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/proposal`) y valid├® su versi├│n en `openspec/changes/official-registry-bootstrap/proposal.md`.
- Ō£ģ Revis├® el c├│digo relevante (`src/core/registry.js`, `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js`, `src/commands/list.js`, `src/commands/add.js`), el PRD y pruebas existentes para basar el dise├▒o en comportamiento real.
- Ō£ģ Cre├® `openspec/changes/official-registry-bootstrap/design.md` con enfoque t├®cnico, decisiones, flujo, contratos y estrategia de validaci├│n.
- Ō£ģ Persist├Ł el artefacto en Engram como `sdd/official-registry-bootstrap/design` y guard├® la decisi├│n arquitect├│nica de mantener el cambio como bootstrap data-only.

## Next Steps
- Ejecutar `sdd-tasks` para desglosar la creaci├│n del repo oficial, seed data y smoke checks.
- Si se busca m├Īs confianza, decidir si se agrega una prueba unitaria opcional en este repo para fijar la prioridad del registry oficial.

## Relevant Files
- openspec/changes/official-registry-bootstrap/design.md ŌĆö dise├▒o t├®cnico creado para el cambio.
- openspec/changes/official-registry-bootstrap/proposal.md ŌĆö propuesta base validada.
- src/core/registry.js ŌĆö contrato real de `RegistrySource`, merge de fuentes y derivaci├│n de URLs.
- src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js ŌĆö fallback adapter y referencia del contrato de source.
- agents-ai-PRD.md ŌĆö estructura y formato esperados del registry oficial.

observations.title: Registry paths must omit .md extension to match getAgentDownloadUrl contract
observations.content:
**What**: Bootstrap completado del registry oficial ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry con 4 agentes seed y registry.json en formato PRD. Zero cambios en el CLI.
**Why**: El CLI ya ten├Ła el cascade multi-registry cableado pero el source primario (agents-ai-registry) devolv├Ła 404. El bootstrap hace real ese source.
**Where**: Repo github.com/ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry (registry.json, 4 agentes .md en agents/*, README.md). Ning├║n cambio en el repo CLI.
**Learned**: El path en registry.json NO debe llevar extensi├│n .md ŌĆö el c├│digo en `getAgentDownloadUrl()` hace `return ${baseUrl}/${relativePath}.md`. Si incluyes .md en el path, la URL resultante ser├Ła `.../frontend-dev.md.md` (doble extensi├│n, roto). El PRD muestra paths con .md pero eso es conceptual ŌĆö el contrato real del c├│digo requiere paths sin extensi├│n.

## Discoveries
- SDD init had stale openspec config and was refreshed to reflect real Vitest support.
- The CLI was already correctly wired for a primary official registry plus community fallback; the missing piece was the upstream registry repo and seed content.
- Multi-registry merge was already implemented in this session, enabling the official registry to take precedence by slug.
- Verify/archive surfaced a follow-up concern: search tests still carry single-source assumptions and should be revisited for the new multi-source reality.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Ran `sdd-init` and refreshed hybrid SDD context for agents-ai.

- Ō£ģ Ran `sdd-verify` and `sdd-archive`; change archived to openspec and engram with PASS status.

## Next Steps
- Update `tests/search.test.js` for the new multi-source registry behavior.
- Consider adding validation CI to the registry repo for `registry.json` and path consistency.
- Consider coverage and cache hardening in future changes.

## Relevant Files

- tests/list.test.js ŌĆö covers multi-source merge precedence.

observations.title: sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/explore
observations.content:
# Exploration: Renombre de paquete npm para publicaci├│n (`agents-ai` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`)

## Current State

El proyecto se llama `agents-ai` tanto como nombre de paquete npm (en `package.json`) como CLI brand (en `program.name()`, mensajes de ayuda, documentaci├│n y escenarios de specs). Existe un paquete ajeno `agents-ai@0.0.2` en npm que impide publicar este proyecto con ese nombre.

Actualmente el proyecto NO est├Ī publicado en npm. El desarrollo se ejecuta localmente con `node bin/agents-ai.js` o `npx` apuntando al path local.

### Identificadores distintos que hoy usan "agents-ai"

He identificado **5 conceptos diferentes** que hoy se llaman "agents-ai" y que NO deber├Łan tratarse como un solo cambio monol├Łtico:

| Concepto | D├│nde | ┬┐Debe cambiar? |
|----------|-------|----------------|
| **npm package name** | `package.json#name` | Ō£ģ MUST ŌĆö es la raz├│n del cambio |
| **CLI program name** | `bin/agents-ai.js#L38` (`.name('agents-ai')`), mensajes help | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ SHOULD ŌĆö es la marca del CLI |
| **Registry source name** | `src/core/registry.js#L105` (`name: 'agents-ai'`), aparece como `source: 'agents-ai'` en datos de agentes | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ COULD ŌĆö es identificador interno |
| **App data directory** | `~/.agents-ai/cache/` en `FileTreeSource.js` | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ COULD ŌĆö riesgo de romper cach├®s existentes |
| **GitHub refs en mocks** | `raw.githubusercontent.com/agents-ai/registry/...` en tests | ­¤öĄ LOW ŌĆö son URLs mock, no reales |

## Affected Areas

### Cambios OBLIGATORIOS (MUST)

| Archivo | L├Łneas | Qu├® cambiar |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| `package.json` | L2 | `"name": "agents-ai"` ŌåÆ `"name": "@ruben-salas20/agents-ai"` |
| `package-lock.json` | L2, L8 | Se regenera con `npm install` |
| `agents-ai-PRD.md` | L39-43, L59, L67, L74, L243, y todas las referencias a `npx agents-ai` | Actualizar para reflejar `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai` |
| `src/core/registry.js` | L105 | `name: 'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `name: '@ruben-salas20/agents-ai'` (el source name del registry oficial) |

### Cambios RECOMENDADOS (SHOULD)

| Archivo | L├Łneas | Qu├® cambiar |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| `src/commands/*.js` | ~12 ocurrencias de `"agents-ai list"`, `"agents-ai installed"`, etc. en mensajes de sugerencia | Actualizar a `"@ruben-salas20/agents-ai list"` o mantener `"agents-ai"` seg├║n decisi├│n de UX |
| `src/core/remover.js` | L89, L102, L160-161 | ├Źdem ŌĆö mensajes de sugerencia |
| `bin/agents-ai.js` | L4, L38 | JSDoc y `.name('agents-ai')` ŌĆö decidir si mantener brand |

### Cambios en TESTS (condicionales)

| Archivo | Naturaleza | Depende de |
|---------|-----------|------------|
| `tests/list.test.js` (~14 refs a `source: 'agents-ai'`) | Source name en fixtures | Decisi├│n sobre registry source name |
| `tests/search.test.js` (~4 refs a `source: 'agents-ai'`) | Source name en fixtures | Decisi├│n sobre registry source name |
| `tests/add.test.js` (L80, L99 ŌĆö `source: 'agents-ai'` + URL mock) | Fixtures + mock URL | Decisi├│n sobre source name |
| `tests/unit/sources/FileTreeSource.test.js` (L457-461 ŌĆö `.agents-ai/cache`) | Path de cach├® | Decisi├│n sobre app data directory |

### Cambios en SPECS (openspec/specs/)

14 spec files referencian `agents-ai add ...` en escenarios Given. Son sintaxis del CLI, no el npm package name. Solo cambiar├Łan si se decide renombrar el CLI brand. **No tocar.** 

### Archivos archivados (openspec/changes/archive/)

M├║ltiples archivos hist├│ricos referencian `agents-ai`. **No modificar** ŌĆö son archivo hist├│rico. El rename aplica solo hacia adelante.

## Approaches

### 1. Enfoque m├Łnimo ŌĆö solo cambiar `name` en `package.json`
- **Qu├®**: Cambiar solo el campo `name` en `package.json`. Todo lo dem├Īs se queda igual.
- **Pros**: M├Łnimo riesgo. El CLI sigue funcionando igual. `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai add ...` funciona con el bin existente.
- **Cons**: Mensajes de ayuda dicen `"agents-ai list"` pero el usuario tuvo que escribir `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai list`. Inconsistencia.
- **Effort**: Low (1 archivo + PRD + regenerar lockfile)
- **Tests afectados**: Ninguno

### 2. Enfoque completo ŌĆö renombrar TODO
- **Qu├®**: Cambiar npm package name + todos los mensajes + program name + source name + tests + specs.
- **Pros**: Consistencia total.
- **Cons**: ~30+ archivos. Riesgo de romper tests. Cambiar program name afecta `--help` output.
- **Effort**: High (~30+ archivos)

### 3. Enfoque pragm├Ītico (RECOMENDADO)
- **Qu├®**: 
  - `package.json#name` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
  - Mensajes de usuario: cambiar a `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai list` etc.
  - Mantener `program.name('agents-ai')` (brand del CLI)
  - RegistrySource name: cambiar a `'official'` (m├Īs sem├Īntico)
  - Mantener `~/.agents-ai/cache/`
  - PRD: actualizar
  - Tests seg├║n cambios de source name
  - Specs: NO tocar
- **Pros**: Balance entre consistencia y esfuerzo.
- **Effort**: Medium (~15-20 archivos)

### Decisi├│n: bin p├║blico
**Mantener `agents-ai` como bin name** en `package.json`. Es el patr├│n est├Īndar (ej: `@angular/cli` expone `ng`). Usuarios ejecutan `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai`.

### Decisi├│n: app data directory
**Mantener `~/.agents-ai/`**. Desacoplado del npm package name. Renombrarlo requerir├Ła migraci├│n de datos.

## Recommendation

**Enfoque pragm├Ītico (Approach 3)** con estas decisiones:

1. Ō£ģ `package.json#name`: `"@ruben-salas20/agents-ai"`
2. Ō£ģ `bin`: mantener `{ "agents-ai": "./bin/agents-ai.js" }` 
3. Ō£ģ `program.name()`: mantener `'agents-ai'`
4. Ō£ģ Mensajes de usuario: `"agents-ai list"` ŌåÆ `"@ruben-salas20/agents-ai list"`
5. Ō£ģ RegistrySource name: `'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `'official'`
6. Ō£ģ Cache dir: mantener `~/.agents-ai/cache/`
7. Ō£ģ PRD: actualizar
8. Ō£ģ Tests: actualizar source name en fixtures
9. ŌØī Specs: NO tocar
10. ŌØī Archivos archivados: NO tocar

## Risks

| Riesgo | Severidad | Mitigaci├│n |
|--------|-----------|------------|
| Colisi├│n con npm cache local del paquete ajeno | Medium | Usar `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai` (scope elimina ambig├╝edad) |
| Source name `'official'` rompe ~18 fixtures en tests | Medium | B├║squeda/reemplazo dirigido en tests |
| RegistrySource.name cambia visualmente en output de `list`/`search` | Low | Los agentes mostrar├Īn `source: "official"` en vez de `source: "agents-ai"` |
| `npm publish` con scope privado por defecto | Medium | Recordar `--access public` |
| package-lock.json regenerado con muchos cambios | Low | Esperado; revisar diff |

## Ready for Proposal

**S├Ł.** Decisiones tomadas. Siguiente fase: `sdd-propose`.

observations.title: sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: package-rename-for-npm-publication

## Intent

Publicar el paquete npm bajo el scope `@ruben-salas20` porque `agents-ai` ya existe en npm e impide la publicaci├│n. El bin p├║blico `agents-ai` se mantiene (funciona igual tras el scope), pero los mensajes de usuario y el source name interno se actualizan para reflejar el nuevo package name.

## Scope

### In Scope
- `package.json#name` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- Regeneraci├│n de `package-lock.json`
- Actualizaci├│n del PRD (`agents-ai-PRD.md`) con nuevos ejemplos `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- Mensajes de usuario en `src/commands/*.js` y `src/core/remover.js` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- RegistrySource `name` en `src/core/registry.js` L105 ŌåÆ `'official'` (sem├Īntico, no incluye el scope)
- Tests que referencian `source: 'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `source: 'official'` (fixtures en `tests/list.test.js`, `tests/search.test.js`, `tests/add.test.js`)
- Smoke test post-cambio

### Out of Scope
- Specs (`openspec/specs/*.md`): la sintaxis CLI `agents-ai add ...` es independiente del package name npm ŌĆö no cambia
- `bin/agents-ai.js` JSDoc y `program.name('agents-ai')`: la marca CLI se mantiene
- App data directory `~/.agents-ai/cache/`: desacoplado del package name
- Archivos archivados en `openspec/changes/archive/`: no se tocan

## Approach

**Enfoque pragm├Ītico (Approach 3 del exploration)**:
1. Cambiar `package.json#name` + regenerar lockfile
2. Actualizar PRD
3. Cambiar `RegistrySource.name` ŌåÆ `'official'` + actualizar fixtures de tests
4. Actualizar mensajes de usuario en commands y remover
5. Smoke test: `node bin/agents-ai.js --help` && `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai --help`

El bin `agents-ai` se mantiene porque npm scoped + bin separado es el patr├│n est├Īndar (ej: `@angular/cli` expone `ng`).

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `package.json` | Modified | `name` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` |
| `package-lock.json` | Modified | Regenerado por `npm install` |
| `agents-ai-PRD.md` | Modified | Actualizar ejemplos npx y referencias |
| `src/core/registry.js` L105 | Modified | `name: 'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `name: 'official'` |
| `src/commands/*.js` | Modified | Mensajes sugerencia ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` |
| `src/core/remover.js` | Modified | Mensajes sugerencia ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` |
| `tests/list.test.js` | Modified | Fixtures: `source: 'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `source: 'official'` |
| `tests/search.test.js` | Modified | Fixtures: `source: 'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `source: 'official'` |
| `tests/add.test.js` | Modified | Fixtures: `source: 'agents-ai'` ŌåÆ `source: 'official'` |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Scoped packages npm son privados por defecto | Medium | Usar `npm publish --access public` al publicar |
| Tests con fixtures outdated post-rename | Medium | B├║squeda/reemplazo dirigida; fixtures, no l├│gica |
| npx cache local tiene versi├│n ajena de `agents-ai` | Low | El scope `@ruben-salas20` evita ambig├╝edad |

## Rollback Plan

1. Reverner `package.json#name` ŌåÆ `agents-ai`
2. Regenerar lockfile: `rm package-lock.json && npm install`
3. Revertir `RegistrySource.name` ŌåÆ `'agents-ai'` en `registry.js`
4. Revertir fixtures de tests a `source: 'agents-ai'`
5. Revertir mensajes en commands y remover.js
6. Revertir cambios en PRD

## Dependencies

- Ninguna dependencia externa. Solo cambios locales de archivos.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npm pack --dry-run` genera un tarball v├Īlido con scope `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- [ ] `node bin/agents-ai.js --help` funciona sin errores
- [ ] `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai --help` muestra el CLI correctamente
- [ ] Todos los tests pasan: `npm test`
- [ ] RegistrySource `name` es `'official'` en runtime
- [ ] Mensajes de usuario muestran `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- [ ] PRD actualizado y consistente con el nuevo package name

observations.title: sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/spec
observations.content:
# Delta for Scoped NPM Publication

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Scoped package identity for npm publication

The system MUST define the npm package identity as a scoped package to avoid name collision in npm public registry.

#### Scenario: Package metadata uses scoped name

- GIVEN the repository package metadata is prepared for publication
- WHEN `package.json` is reviewed for publish identity
- THEN `name` SHALL be `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- AND lockfile metadata SHALL remain consistent with that package name

#### Scenario: Publication flow declares public scoped access

- GIVEN the package will be published to npm
- WHEN maintainers run the publication command
- THEN the process SHOULD use public scoped publication settings
- AND documentation or release instructions SHOULD indicate public access requirement

### Requirement: Public CLI binary compatibility

The system MUST keep the executable CLI command name `agents-ai` while allowing scoped package invocation.

#### Scenario: Local CLI entrypoint remains compatible

- GIVEN a local checkout of the repository
- WHEN the user executes `node bin/agents-ai.js --help`
- THEN the CLI SHALL respond successfully
- AND command branding SHALL remain `agents-ai`

#### Scenario: Scoped npx invocation resolves to same CLI

- GIVEN the package is available as `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- WHEN the user runs `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai --help`
- THEN npm SHALL resolve the scoped package
- AND the invoked binary SHALL be usable as `agents-ai`

### Requirement: User-facing guidance reflects scoped invocation

The system MUST align user-visible documentation and in-app guidance with scoped npm invocation while preserving CLI syntax semantics.

#### Scenario: Documentation examples use scoped npx form

- GIVEN user documentation and PRD examples
- WHEN invocation examples are shown for package execution
- THEN examples SHALL use `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- AND examples SHALL continue to show CLI subcommands with `agents-ai <command>` semantics

#### Scenario: Suggestion strings reference scoped package

- GIVEN command error/help suggestions that currently reference package installation/execution
- WHEN suggestion text is displayed
- THEN the text SHALL reference `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` for package invocation
- AND it SHALL NOT imply unscoped npm package installation

### Requirement: Internal source naming transition without CLI regressions

The system MUST support updating internal registry source labeling from `agents-ai` to `official` without changing CLI behavior.

#### Scenario: Runtime source label uses semantic value

- GIVEN registry-derived agent metadata is produced at runtime
- WHEN source labeling is assigned for official registry entries
- THEN source label SHALL be `official`
- AND CLI command behavior SHALL remain unchanged

#### Scenario: Relevant tests protect behavior after rename

- GIVEN test fixtures previously asserting `source: 'agents-ai'`
- WHEN the rename change is applied
- THEN relevant tests SHALL assert `source: 'official'`
- AND the test suite SHALL continue passing for affected command flows

observations.title: Defined spec domain for scoped npm publication
observations.content:
**What**: Defin├Ł el dominio de spec `scoped-npm-publication` como NUEVO (ADDED requirements) al no existir secci├│n `Capabilities` en la propuesta ni spec base equivalente en `openspec/specs`.
**Why**: El cambio afecta publicaci├│n npm, mensajes de invocaci├│n y etiquetado interno de source, sin modificar la sintaxis funcional de comandos existente.
**Where**: `sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/package-rename-for-npm-publication/specs/scoped-npm-publication/spec.md`, `openspec/specs/*`.
**Learned**: Para cambios de packaging/distribuci├│n conviene un dominio separado para evitar mezclar requisitos de ejecuci├│n CLI ya cubiertos por specs hist├│ricas.

observations.title: Detected source contract mismatch for official registry
observations.content:
**What**: Detect├® que el spec activo `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` todav├Ła exige que los agentes del registry oficial expongan `source: "agents-ai"`.
**Why**: El dise├▒o de `package-rename-for-npm-publication` propone cambiar ese identificador interno a `official`, lo que introduce un posible desalineamiento entre specs vigentes e implementaci├│n futura.
**Where**: `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md`, `src/core/registry.js`, `sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/design.md`
**Learned**: El rename del package npm no es solo branding; tambi├®n toca un contrato interno de tests y specs alrededor del campo `source`.

observations.title: sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/design
observations.content:
# Design: package-rename-for-npm-publication

## Technical Approach

Aplicar el rename SOLO en la superficie de publicaci├│n npm y en los identificadores internos estrictamente acoplados a ese cambio. El paquete pasa a `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`, el bin p├║blico sigue siendo `agents-ai`, y el c├│digo mantiene la marca CLI local mientras corrige mensajes y fixtures que hoy dependen del nombre viejo.

## Architecture Decisions

| Decision | Options | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Package name | keep `agents-ai` / scoped rename | `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` | Resuelve la colisi├│n en npm sin tocar el entrypoint ni la UX base del CLI. |
| Public bin | rename bin / keep bin | Keep `bin.agents-ai` | npm scoped package + unscoped bin es patr├│n normal; evita romper `program.name('agents-ai')` y ayuda local. |
| Registry source label | use scoped package / keep `agents-ai` / semantic alias | `official` | `source` en runtime es identificador interno de procedencia, NO branding npm. As├Ł evitamos acoplar datos internos al scope de publicaci├│n. |
| Non-public identifiers | rename cache, archived docs, specs, CLI brand / defer | Defer | `~/.agents-ai/cache/`, `program.name('agents-ai')` y archivos hist├│ricos representan compatibilidad o branding estable; renombrarlos ahora mezcla concerns y aumenta riesgo. |

## Data Flow

`npm package` ŌåÆ resuelve `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` ŌåÆ ejecuta bin `agents-ai` ŌåÆ comandos muestran hints con package scoped ŌåÆ `registry.js` normaliza agentes con `source: 'official'` ŌåÆ tests validan ese contrato.

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `package.json` | Modify | `name` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`; mantener `bin.agents-ai`. |
| `package-lock.json` | Modify | Regenerar metadatos tras cambio de package name. |
| `agents-ai-PRD.md` | Modify | Actualizar ejemplos `npx` al package scoped. |
| `src/core/registry.js` | Modify | `DEFAULT_SOURCES[0].name` ŌåÆ `official`; ajustar comentarios/examples que asumen `agents-ai`. |
| `src/commands/add.js` | Modify | Cambiar hints `Run "agents-ai list"` por `Run "npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai list"`. |
| `src/commands/installed.js` | Modify | Cambiar hints equivalentes para instalaci├│n/listado. |
| `src/commands/search.js` | Modify | Cambiar hint de descubrimiento al comando scoped. |
| `src/core/remover.js` | Modify | Cambiar hints `installed/list` al comando scoped. |
| `tests/list.test.js` | Modify | Fixtures y expectations `source: 'official'`; conservar community fallback igual. |
| `tests/search.test.js` | Modify | Fixtures/expectations de prioridad y merge con `source: 'official'`. |
| `tests/add.test.js` | Modify | Fixture `sampleAgent.source` ŌåÆ `official`; URL mock no cambia porque depende del repo, no del source label. |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```js
// package.json
{ "name": "@ruben-salas20/agents-ai", "bin": { "agents-ai": "./bin/agents-ai.js" } }

// normalized agent from official registry
{ source: 'official' }
```

No cambia la firma p├║blica de comandos. Cambia el contrato interno del campo `source` para el registry oficial.

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|---|---|---|
| Unit | `RegistrySource` normalization y lookup por source | Ajustar fixtures en `list/search/add` para `official`. |
| Integration | CLI help y comandos siguen resolviendo bin | `node bin/agents-ai.js --help`. |
| Packaging | Publicaci├│n npm scoped | `npm pack --dry-run` y smoke `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai --help`. |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration de datos. Rollout en un solo paso: cambiar package name, regenerar lockfile, actualizar strings/tests, validar pack y publicar con `npm publish --access public`.

## Open Questions

- [ ] El spec activo `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` todav├Ła exige `source: "agents-ai"`; verify deber├Ī decidir si ese contrato se actualiza en un cambio posterior o dentro de este cambio.

observations.title: Session summary: agents-ai
observations.content:
## Goal
Crear el dise├▒o t├®cnico SDD para el cambio `package-rename-for-npm-publication`.

## Instructions
- Usar evidencia del proposal/exploration y del c├│digo actual.
- Guardar descubrimientos importantes en Engram para el proyecto `agents-ai`.
- Devolver el contrato est├Īndar SDD: status, executive_summary, artifacts, next_recommended, risks, skill_resolution.

## Discoveries
- El paquete actual usa `name: "agents-ai"` y ya mantiene el bin p├║blico desacoplado en `bin.agents-ai`, as├Ł que el rename scoped NO requiere cambiar el entrypoint.
- `src/core/registry.js` usa `name: 'agents-ai'` como identificador interno del source oficial; cambiarlo a `official` impacta fixtures y expectations de tests, no la URL del registry.
- El spec activo `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` todav├Ła exige `source: "agents-ai"`, lo que introduce una posible desalineaci├│n con el dise├▒o propuesto.
- `FileTreeSource` y sus tests siguen acoplados a `~/.agents-ai/cache/`; dejarlos fuera del rename evita migraciones de cach├®.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Le├Ł el proposal desde `sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/proposal.md`, el exploration y el c├│digo relevante (`package.json`, `bin/agents-ai.js`, `src/core/registry.js`, commands, tests, PRD, spec activo).
- Ō£ģ Redact├® el dise├▒o t├®cnico en `sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/design.md` cubriendo scope scoped package, bin estable, cambios internos, exclusiones de rename, validaci├│n y notas de publicaci├│n.
- Ō£ģ Persist├Ł el artifact en Engram como `sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/design`.
- Ō£ģ Guard├® en Engram el descubrimiento sobre el desalineamiento del spec `registry-bootstrap` con el nuevo `source: 'official'`.

## Next Steps
- Ejecutar `sdd-tasks` para desglosar implementaci├│n.
- Confirmar en verify si el spec `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` debe actualizarse dentro de este cambio o en uno posterior.

## Relevant Files
- sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/design.md ŌĆö dise├▒o t├®cnico del cambio.
- sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/proposal.md ŌĆö alcance y approach base.
- sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/exploration.md ŌĆö decisiones y alternativas evaluadas.
- package.json ŌĆö package name actual y bin p├║blico.
- bin/agents-ai.js ŌĆö marca CLI mantenida con `program.name('agents-ai')`.
- src/core/registry.js ŌĆö source oficial actual y contrato interno de `source`.
- openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md ŌĆö spec activo con posible conflicto de `source: "agents-ai"`.

observations.title: Tasks breakdown for package-rename-for-npm-publication
observations.content:
**What**: Created the implementation task checklist for renaming the npm package to `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` while keeping the public CLI bin `agents-ai`.
**Why**: To unblock npm publication and align internal source identifiers with the scoped package name.
**Where**: `sdd/package-rename-for-npm-publication/tasks` (filesystem + Engram).
**Learned**: The project uses a custom `sdd/` layout rather than `openspec/`.

observations.title: Applied package-rename-for-npm-publication change
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented all 14 tasks across 5 phases for renaming npm package from `agents-ai` to `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`.

**Why**: `agents-ai` already exists in npm, blocking publication. Scoped package resolves collision while keeping bin name `agents-ai`.

**Where**:
- `package.json` ŌĆö `name` ŌåÆ `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`; `bin.agents-ai` kept
- `package-lock.json` ŌĆö regenerated
- `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö `DEFAULT_SOURCES[0].name` ŌåÆ `'official'`; updated JSDoc examples
- `src/commands/add.js` ŌĆö 3 hint strings ŌåÆ `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai`
- `src/commands/installed.js` ŌĆö 1 hint string ŌåÆ scoped
- `src/commands/search.js` ŌĆö 1 hint string ŌåÆ scoped
- `src/commands/remove.js` ŌĆö 1 hint string ŌåÆ scoped
- `src/core/remover.js` ŌĆö 4 hint strings ŌåÆ scoped
- `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö 13 fixture/expectation replacements `agents-ai` ŌåÆ `official`; 3 additional for source names
- `tests/search.test.js` ŌĆö 4 fixture replacements + 1 hint expectation; added `global.fetch = vi.fn()` to 4 describe blocks to fix real HTTP contamination
- `tests/add.test.js` ŌĆö 1 fixture replacement + 1 hint expectation
- `tests/remove.test.js` ŌĆö 1 hint expectation
- `tests/remover.test.js` ŌĆö 2 hint expectations
- `agents-ai-PRD.md` ŌĆö All `npx agents-ai` ŌåÆ `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai`; updated source label docs

**Learned**: 
1. Node 18+ native `fetch` causes search tests to hit real GitHub API via DEFAULT_SOURCES. Fixed by adding `global.fetch = vi.fn()` in beforeEach blocks for test isolation. This is a pre-existing defect in the test suite, not caused by the rename.
2. `remover.js` L160 had `\n` inside the string literal, making `replaceAll:true` miss it ŌĆö required a separate targeted edit.
3. RegistrySource._normalize() overrides the `source` field from raw data with `this.name`, so some fixture `source: 'agents-ai'` values in tests are dead code (overridden at normalization time). Tests still pass.
4. npm scoped packages generate tarball names like `ruben-salas20-agents-ai-0.1.0.tgz`.

**Verification**:
- `npm test`: 232 tests, all passed Ō£ģ

observations.title: Node native fetch contaminates vitest search tests via DEFAULT_SOURCES
observations.content:
**What**: search.test.js and other tests using DEFAULT_SOURCES make real HTTP calls to the official registry URL because Node 18+ has native `global.fetch`. This causes test contamination ŌĆö real registry data merges with mock data.

**Why**: `searchAgents()` and `listAgents()` fall back to DEFAULT_SOURCES when no custom sources are provided. The first source is `RegistrySource({name: 'official', url: '...'})` which calls `fetch(this.url)`. If `global.fetch` is not mocked, it makes a real HTTP call to GitHub.

**Where**: `tests/search.test.js` ŌĆö describes: 'matching', 'return shape', 'source merge behavior', 'no-results UX integration'

**Learned**: Fix applied: add `global.fetch = vi.fn()` to `beforeEach` in every describe block that calls functions using DEFAULT_SOURCES without passing custom sources. `vi.fn()()` returns `undefined`, causing the real RegistrySource to fail silently (error caught in fetchAndMerge's catch block). This is a pre-existing defect ŌĆö the tests were accidentally passing when `global.fetch` was unavailable or when real registry data happened to not interfere with assertions.

observations.title: Session summary: agents-ai
observations.content:
## Goal
Rename the npm package to a scoped name so npx resolves this project instead of the conflicting public package.

## Instructions
- User accepted the scoped-package strategy and wanted to continue through SDD in automatic + hybrid mode.
- Keep the public bin name `agents-ai` if possible.

## Discoveries
- `npx agents-ai` was resolving a different published package (`agents-ai@0.0.2`) built with oclif; this repo was never being executed from npm.
- The safest product move is `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` as package name while keeping `bin.agents-ai` and CLI branding unchanged.
- During apply, a latent test issue surfaced: some search tests were leaking to real network through Node native `fetch`; this was fixed by explicitly mocking `global.fetch`.
- There is now a low-severity spec drift: `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` still mentions `source: "agents-ai"` while runtime now uses `source: "official"`.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Ran full SDD cycle for `package-rename-for-npm-publication` in automatic + hybrid mode.
- Ō£ģ Renamed package to `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` and kept public bin as `agents-ai`.
- Ō£ģ Updated internal official registry source naming to `official`, adjusted command hints, PRD examples, and affected tests.
- Ō£ģ Verified with `npm test` (232 passing), `npm pack --dry-run`, local CLI help, and archived the change.

## Next Steps
- Publish with `npm publish --access public` when ready.
- Update `openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` to replace `source: "agents-ai"` with `source: "official"`.
- Optionally add README/public release docs for the new scoped invocation.

## Relevant Files
- package.json ŌĆö package renamed to `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` while keeping the `agents-ai` bin.
- package-lock.json ŌĆö regenerated after rename.
