observations.content:
**What**: Verifiqu├® la preparaci├│n de publicaci├│n npm del paquete scoped `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`.
**Why**: El usuario pidi├│ verificar el siguiente paso para publicaci├│n tras resolver la colisi├│n de nombre.

Para publicar habr├Ī que usar `npm publish --access public`.

observations.title: Prepared package but publish blocked by npm 2FA
observations.content:
**What**: Prepar├® el paquete para publicaci├│n p├║blica a├▒adiendo README de ra├Łz y metadata p├║blica (`repository`, `homepage`, `bugs`) y luego intent├® publicar `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai@0.1.0` en npm.
**Why**: El usuario pidi├│ dejar el paquete listo y subirlo tras resolver la colisi├│n de nombre.
**Where**: README.md, package.json, `npm pack --dry-run`, `npm publish --access public`.
**Learned**: La publicaci├│n fall├│ con `E403` porque npm exige 2FA o un granular access token con bypass 2FA para publicar. El empaquetado y tests est├Īn correctos; el bloqueo ya no es t├®cnico del paquete sino de seguridad de la cuenta npm.

observations.title: Session summary: agents-ai
observations.content:
## Goal
Prepare the scoped npm package for public publication and publish it if possible.

## Instructions
- User had already completed npm authentication with `npm adduser`.
- Create a public-facing README and then publish the package.

## Discoveries
- npm authentication was valid (`npm whoami` returned `ruben-salas20`), so the previous auth blocker was resolved.
- The package was technically healthy: `npm pack --dry-run` and full tests both passed after adding README and public metadata.
- Publication is now blocked by npm account security, not by the package: npm returned `E403` requiring 2FA or a granular access token with bypass 2FA enabled.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Added root `README.md` with install/usage instructions for `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai`.
- Ō£ģ Updated `package.json` with `repository`, `homepage`, and `bugs` metadata.
- Ō£ģ Verified package contents with `npm pack --dry-run` and confirmed README is included.
- Ō£ģ Ran full test suite successfully (232 passing).
- Ō£ģ Attempted `npm publish --access public`; publish failed due to npm 2FA/token policy (`E403`).

## Next Steps
- Publish again with an OTP if your npm account uses 2FA for publishing, or use a granular access token with bypass 2FA enabled.
- After successful publish, verify from a clean shell with `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai --help` and `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai list`.

## Relevant Files
- README.md ŌĆö public package landing page for npm.
- package.json ŌĆö scoped package metadata plus repository/homepage/bugs fields.
- bin/agents-ai.js ŌĆö public CLI entrypoint included in the tarball.

observations.title: v0.4 Flask app ŌĆö path resolution gotcha
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed incorrect `parents[N]` index in all v0.4 route modules that inject v0.2/v0.3 into sys.path.

**Why**: The spec suggested `parents[4]` for repo root from dashboard/routes.py but the actual depth is 3 (dashboard ŌåÆ app ŌåÆ v0.4 ŌåÆ repo_root).

**Where**: `v0.4/app/dashboard/routes.py`, `v0.4/app/runs/routes.py`, `v0.4/app/runs/jobs.py`

**Learned**: Always verify parents depth from the concrete file path. For any file in `v0.4/app/<blueprint>/file.py`: parents[0]=blueprint, parents[1]=app, parents[2]=v0.4, parents[3]=repo_root. For `v0.4/app/__init__.py`: parents[0]=app, parents[1]=v0.4, parents[2]=repo_root. RunRepository does not have a `latest_run()` method ŌĆö use inline SQL instead.

observations.title: v0.4 Flask skeleton implementado y verificado
observations.content:
**What**: Implementaci├│n completa del skeleton de v0.4 Flask para VAECOS Tracking ŌĆö auth, dashboard, runs, Excel import, user management.

**Why**: Migrar de http.server stdlib (single-user local) a Flask multi-usuario desplegable en VPS.

**Where**:
- `v0.4/` ŌĆö directorio nuevo completo (30+ archivos)
- `v0.4/server.py` ŌĆö entry point dev/prod (waitress en prod)
- `v0.4/config.py` ŌĆö V04Settings + load_settings()
- `v0.4/app/__init__.py` ŌĆö create_app() factory
- `v0.4/app/auth/` ŌĆö login/logout/change-password + @login_required + @admin_required + UserRepository
- `v0.4/app/dashboard/routes.py` ŌĆö 11 GET routes portadas desde v0.3
- `v0.4/app/runs/` ŌĆö job dispatch + POST /run/new + CSV export + notas
- `v0.4/app/import_guides/` ŌĆö ExcelParser + /import + /import/confirm
- `v0.4/app/users/` ŌĆö CRUD usuarios admin
- `v0.4/templates/` ŌĆö 17 templates Jinja2 + macros + sidebar + flash
- `v0.4/static/css/styles.css` ŌĆö design system CSS (735 l├Łneas, Geist fonts)
- `v0.2/vaecos_v02/storage/db.py` ŌĆö tablas users + import_log + WAL mode pragma
- `.env` ŌĆö agregadas variables V04_* y FLASK_SECRET_KEY
- `iniciar_v04.bat` ŌĆö launcher Windows

**Learned**:
- `v0.4/` tiene un punto en el nombre, no es importable directamente. server.py agrega `v0.4/` a sys.path, y dentro se usa `from app import create_app`.
- `v0.4/app/__init__.py` resuelve REPO_ROOT como `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]` (parents: [0]=app, [1]=v0.4, [2]=repo root).
- DashboardRepository de v0.3 se importa directamente en dashboard/routes.py agregando `v0.3/` al sys.path.
- curl sin Content-Type header retorna 405 en POST de Flask ŌĆö siempre usar `-H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"`.
- Bootstrap user: credenciales en .env como V04_BOOTSTRAP_EMAIL + V04_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD. Se crea solo si users table est├Ī vac├Ła.
- 225/225 tests de v0.2 siguen pasando despu├®s de agregar tablas users + import_log + WAL pragma.
- Verificado: 24 rutas registradas, todos los endpoints retornan 200, auth flow completo funciona.

observations.title: Session summary: vaecos-tracking
observations.content:
## Goal
Planificar e implementar Fase 1 de VAECOS Tracking: migrar de http.server stdlib a Flask con auth, Excel import y user management.

## Instructions
- Conventional commits, no Co-Authored-By
- Responder en espa├▒ol
- No hacer build despu├®s de cambios
- WAL mode en SQLite para VPS readiness

## Discoveries
- `v0.4/` tiene punto en el nombre ŌĆö no importable directamente. server.py inserta `v0.4/` en sys.path y usa `from app import create_app`.
- REPO_ROOT desde `v0.4/app/__init__.py` es `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]` (no parents[3]).
- DashboardRepository de v0.3 se reutiliza directamente sin modificar ŌĆö solo agregar `v0.3/` al sys.path en dashboard/routes.py.
- curl necesita `-H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"` para POST a Flask ŌĆö sin ese header retorna 405.
- WAL pragma se agrega en `connect()` no en `init_db()` ŌĆö aplica a TODAS las conexiones.
- Bootstrap user se crea en create_app() solo cuando users table est├Ī vac├Ła.
- Credenciales bootstrap: `V04_BOOTSTRAP_EMAIL=admin@vaecos.com`, `V04_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD=vaecos2026` (en .env).
- `FLASK_SECRET_KEY=vaecos-dev-key-change-in-prod-2026` agregado a .env.

## Accomplished
- Ō£ģ Documentaci├│n completa: PRD v2.0, roadmap-estrategico.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, DESIGN.md
- Ō£ģ CLAUDE.md creado con arquitectura, comandos y rutas al dise├▒o
- Ō£ģ Design system recuperado: docs/design-system/ (styles.css 735L, 9 JSX screens)
- Ō£ģ Plan de Fase 1 aprobado y guardado
- Ō£ģ A1: DB migrations ŌĆö tablas `users` + `import_log` + WAL pragma en db.py (225 tests OK)
- Ō£ģ Dependencias instaladas: flask 3.1, bcrypt 5.0, openpyxl 3.1, waitress 3.0
- Ō£ģ A2-D: v0.4 completo ŌĆö 30+ archivos, 24 rutas, 5 blueprints
- Ō£ģ Auth: /login, /logout, /change-password, @login_required, @admin_required
- Ō£ģ Dashboard: 11 GET routes portadas (/, /attention, /analytics, etc.)
- Ō£ģ Runs: POST /run/new, progreso, CSV export, notas
- Ō£ģ Import: ExcelParser flexible + /import + /import/confirm ŌåÆ import_log
- Ō£ģ Users: list, create, toggle (admin only)
- Ō£ģ Templates Jinja2 con design system CSS
- Ō£ģ iniciar_v04.bat en ra├Łz del repo
- Ō£ģ Verificado: login flow, 24 rutas 200, HTML structure correcto

## Next Steps
- Revisar visualmente en browser todas las pantallas (ejecutar `iniciar_v04.bat`)
- Identificar y corregir errores reportados por la operadora (estados propuestos, visualizaci├│n de gu├Łas)
- Probar upload de Excel real del ERP
- Probar Nueva corrida desde v0.4 (run dispatch)
- Ajustes visuales de UI si hay diferencias con el design system
- Cuando todo est├® validado localmente: deploy en VPS Hostinger (~$5/mes)

## Relevant Files
- `v0.4/app/__init__.py` ŌĆö create_app() factory, bootstrap seed, context processor
- `v0.4/app/auth/routes.py` ŌĆö login/logout/change-password
- `v0.4/app/auth/user_repo.py` ŌĆö UserRepository (CRUD users table)
- `v0.4/app/dashboard/routes.py` ŌĆö todos los GET routes del dashboard
- `v0.4/app/runs/jobs.py` ŌĆö dispatch_run() con thread daemon
- `v0.4/app/import_guides/parser.py` ŌĆö ExcelParser con header detection
- `v0.4/templates/base.html` ŌĆö layout principal con sidebar
- `v0.4/templates/partials/sidebar.html` ŌĆö nav groups, user card
- `v0.4/static/css/styles.css` ŌĆö design system CSS (Geist, warm neutrals, brand red)
- `v0.2/vaecos_v02/storage/db.py` ŌĆö migraciones users + import_log + WAL pragma
- `.env` ŌĆö credenciales bootstrap + FLASK_SECRET_KEY + V04_* vars
- `iniciar_v04.bat` ŌĆö launcher Windows para v0.4

observations.title: bajador-brillo MVP construido en Go + Bubble Tea
observations.content:
**What**: MVP completo de bajador-brillo ŌĆö control de brillo de monitores externos en Windows v├Ła DDC/CI. CLI con cobra (list/get/set, soporta +N/-N relativos) + TUI Bubble Tea con debounce de 150ms.

**Why**: usuario quer├Ła herramienta simple para controlar brillo de monitor externo. Eligi├│ Go + Bubble Tea para practicar TUIs.

**Where**:
- `main.go` ŌĆö entry point, incluye `shieldNegativeDelta` para que cobra no confunda `-10` con flag.
- `cmd/` ŌĆö root.go, list.go, get.go, set.go.
- `internal/brightness/` ŌĆö Controller interface, controller_windows.go con syscalls a dxva2.dll (EnumDisplayMonitors, GetPhysicalMonitorsFromHMONITOR, GetMonitorBrightness, SetMonitorBrightness, DestroyPhysicalMonitors), MockController para tests.
- `internal/tui/` ŌĆö model/update/view con lipgloss styling.

**Learned**:
- Cobra interpreta `-10` como flag desconocido. Soluci├│n: pre-procesar os.Args y inyectar `--` despu├®s de `set` cuando el siguiente arg matchea `^-\d+$`.
- DDC/CI reporta min/cur/max y hay que normalizar a 0-100. Algunos monitores reportan max==min, en ese caso devolver ErrNotSupported.
- Wrapper a dxva2.dll a mano (~150 l├Łneas) m├Īs simple que adoptar librer├Łas de terceros como niluan304/ddcci.
- Go 1.26.2 instalado v├Ła `winget install GoLang.Go`.
- Test de Bubble Tea con `Update()` directo es suficiente para flujo b├Īsico ŌĆö no hizo falta teatest.

observations.title: Session summary: bajador-brillo
observations.content:
## Goal
Crear bajador-brillo: herramienta para controlar el brillo de monitor externo en Windows. Iteraci├│n: PRD ŌåÆ MVP funcional.

## Discoveries
- Cobra trata `-10` como flag desconocido; mitigaci├│n: pre-procesar os.Args inyectando `--` antes del valor negativo despu├®s de `set`.
- DDC/CI v├Ła dxva2.dll requiere normalizar (cur-min)*100/(max-min) ŌĆö los monitores no siempre reportan rango 0-100.
- Go 1.26.2 instalado por winget, ruta default `C:\Program Files\Go\bin\go.exe` (la PATH del shell del agente no se refresca tras instalaci├│n; usar ruta absoluta).
- Bubble Tea + Lipgloss + Cobra integran sin fricci├│n. Tests de TUI con Update() directo (sin teatest) bastan para verificar transiciones.

## Accomplished
- PRD aprobado en `C:\Users\ruben\.claude\plans\la-idea-es-crear-shimmering-orbit.md`.
- Proyecto Go inicializado: m├│dulo `github.com/vaecosshop/bajador-brillo`.
- Wrapper DDC/CI a mano sobre dxva2.dll (~150 l├Łneas).
- CLI cobra: `list`, `get`, `set <abs|+N|-N>`, flag `-m/--monitor`. Exit codes 0/1/2.
- TUI Bubble Tea: navegaci├│n monitor con Ōåæ/Ōåō, brillo con ŌåÉ/ŌåÆ (┬▒5) y shift+ŌåÉ/ŌåÆ (┬▒1), debounce 150ms, recarga con `r`.
- Mock controller para tests.
- Tests pasando: `go test ./...` ok en cmd/, internal/brightness/, internal/tui/.
- Verificaci├│n end-to-end: detecta monitor real, set/get coherentes (50ŌåÆ50, +20ŌåÆ70, -10ŌåÆ60, 150ŌåÆexit 2).
- README completo con uso, requisitos, troubleshooting.

## Next Steps
- Hotkeys globales (AutoHotkey llamando a `brillo set +10`/`-10`).
- Soporte monitor interno de laptop v├Ła WMI.
- Animaci├│n de transici├│n suave (interpolaci├│n N pasos).
- Port a Linux con i2c-dev/ddcutil detr├Īs de la misma interfaz Controller.

## Relevant Files
- `D:\Mis Archivos\Documentos\Proyectos\bajador-brillo\main.go` ŌĆö entry + shieldNegativeDelta.
- `cmd/{root,list,get,set}.go` ŌĆö subcomandos cobra.
- `internal/brightness/controller.go` ŌĆö interface y errores.
- `internal/brightness/controller_windows.go` ŌĆö syscalls dxva2.
- `internal/brightness/controller_mock.go` ŌĆö mock para tests.
- `internal/tui/{model,update,view}.go` ŌĆö Bubble Tea + Lipgloss.
- `README.md` ŌĆö uso, troubleshooting.
- `C:\Users\ruben\.claude\plans\la-idea-es-crear-shimmering-orbit.md` ŌĆö PRD aprobado.

observations.title: Agregadas capas de contraste y gamma a bajador-brillo
observations.content:
**What**: Extendido bajador-brillo con dos capas adicionales de atenuaci├│n: contraste DDC/CI (VCP 0x12 v├Ła GetMonitorContrast/SetMonitorContrast en dxva2.dll) y gamma ramp del GPU (gdi32!SetDeviceGammaRamp/GetDeviceGammaRamp). TUI refactorizada con foco por Tab que cicla entre Brillo HW / Contraste / Gamma SW. CLI con subcomandos `brillo contrast get|set` y `brillo dim get|set`.

**Why**: el usuario report├│ que con DDC/CI en 0% la pantalla segu├Ła brillando demasiado. El "0%" del firmware del monitor no apaga el backlight ŌĆö es el m├Łnimo de f├Ībrica. Necesitaba bajar m├Īs para uso nocturno.

**Where**:
- `internal/brightness/controller.go` ŌĆö interface extendida con GetContrast/SetContrast.
- `internal/brightness/controller_windows.go` ŌĆö implementaci├│n de contraste con dxva2.
- `internal/dim/dim.go` ŌĆö nueva interfaz Dimmer.
- `internal/dim/dim_windows.go` ŌĆö gamma ramp con gdi32, GetDC(NULL) para pantalla principal, ramp lineal escalada por value/100.
- `internal/dim/dim_mock.go` ŌĆö mock para tests.
- `cmd/contrast.go`, `cmd/dim.go` ŌĆö subcomandos cobra.
- `internal/tui/{model,update,view}.go` ŌĆö refactor con focusArea (Brightness/Contrast/Gamma), Tab cicla foco, applyKey para tracking debounce per (├Īrea, monitor).
- `main.go` ŌĆö `shieldNegativeDelta` ya cubre `contrast set` y `dim set` porque busca por nombre "set".

**Learned**:
- Windows 10/11 clampea SetDeviceGammaRamp ŌĆö el rango efectivo real es ~40-100%, no 0-100. Para negro real hace falta overlay de ventana.
- GetMonitorContrast falla en monitores que no implementan VCP 0x12 (t├Łpico en Generic PnP / paneles internos de laptop). El error es "valor del c├│digo VCP continuo supera el m├Īximo" ŌĆö propagar como ErrNotSupported.
- Identidad de gamma ramp 16-bit: ramp[c][i] = i*257 (porque 255*257 = 65535).
- Gamma round-trip tiene drift de 1: setear 90 lee como 89 por redondeo de uint16. Aceptable.
- En la TUI, usar focusArea + applyKey{area, monitor} compacta tres flujos de debounce en uno.

observations.title: Cobra mousetrap intercepta doble-click desde Explorer en Windows
observations.content:
**What**: Cobra integra `github.com/inconshreveable/mousetrap` que detecta cuando un binario Go se lanza desde Explorer (no desde terminal) en Windows y muestra un aviso "This is a command line tool". Para apps cuya TUI es el modo principal, esto bloquea el caso de uso "doble-click ŌåÆ ventana con TUI".

**Why**: el usuario quer├Ła un acceso directo en el escritorio para abrir bajador-brillo sin pasar por la terminal.

**Where**: `cmd/root.go` ŌĆö agregar `cobra.MousetrapHelpText = ""` en `init()` antes de registrar comandos.

**Learned**: setearlo a string vac├Ło desactiva el check globalmente. Las apps console-mode en Windows abren su propia ventana de consola al ser lanzadas desde Explorer, as├Ł que la TUI funciona naturalmente con doble-click una vez desactivado mousetrap.

observations.title: Published @ruben-salas20/agents-ai to npm (0.1.1)
observations.content:
**What**: El paquete scoped `@ruben-salas20/agents-ai` qued├│ publicado en npm en versi├│n `0.1.1` (latest). `npx @ruben-salas20/agents-ai` ya resuelve al CLI publicado.

**Why**: Cierra el bloqueo de E403/2FA reportado en la sesi├│n anterior y desbloquea el ciclo p├║blico del producto.

**Where**: Registro npm p├║blico; `package.json` del repo local apunta a la misma identidad scoped.

**Learned**: La versi├│n salt├│ de la 0.1.0 (fallida por 2FA) a 0.1.1 al volver a publicar ŌĆö npm no permite re-usar versi├│n aunque la publicaci├│n previa haya fallado.

observations.title: Decision: scale `list` via client-side discovery (paginaci├│n + TUI fuzzy + categor├Łas/tags)
observations.content:
**What**: Para escalar el comando `list` del CLI agents-ai, vamos por la ruta pragm├Ītica client-side antes de moverse a b├║squeda server-side. Tres capas:
1. Registry-side: agregar `category` y `tags` al schema de cada agente en el ├Łndice oficial (JSON est├Ītico sigue).
2. CLI no-interactivo: `list --category`, `--tag`, `--query`, `--limit`, `--page` para scripts/CI.
3. CLI interactivo: `list` sin flags abre TUI con fuzzy search en vivo, reutilizando el patr├│n de `install-selection-tui` ya existente. Primero mostrar curados/featured.

**Why**: El usuario plante├│ que volcar todos los agentes no escala (proyectado a miles). La opci├│n server-side escala mejor pero requiere endpoint din├Īmico y m├Īs trabajo; la client-side aprovecha que el ├Łndice est├Ītico a├║n es viable a cientos/miles de entradas y permite avanzar r├Īpido. Cuando duela el tama├▒o del JSON est├Ītico, se migra a search endpoint.

**Where**: Afecta `src/commands/list.js` (o equivalente), schema del registry oficial, posible nuevo m├│dulo TUI, y espec├Łfica generaci├│n del ├Łndice.

**Learned**: El repo ya tiene un componente TUI de selecci├│n (`install-selection-tui` archivado) ŌĆö debe reutilizarse en lugar de crear uno nuevo, para consistencia visual/UX y para no duplicar dependencias.

observations.title: sdd/list-discovery-scaling/explore
observations.content:
## Exploration: list-discovery-scaling

**Project:** agents-ai
**Date:** 2026-05-07
**Status:** Complete
**Change:** list-discovery-scaling
**Strict TDD Mode:** ACTIVE (vitest run ŌĆö must write tests before implementation)

---

### Current State

#### `list` command (`src/commands/list.js`)

The command calls `listAgents({ category })` from `src/core/registry.js` and renders agents grouped by category using chalk + console.log. Current flags: only `-c/--category <name>`. No `--tag`, `--query`, `--limit`, `--page`, no TUI mode. The rendering loop prints displayName (if available) with the slug in dim, plus version and source. The output is purely text ŌĆö no interactive elements.

#### `search` command (`src/commands/search.js`)

Calls `searchAgents(query)` which does case-insensitive substring matching across `name`, `displayName`, `category`, `description`, and `tags`. Returns a `Map<category, Agent[]>`. The rendering is identical to `list`. This means `search` ALREADY covers the query-matching use case ŌĆö the new `list --query` flag should reuse `searchAgents()` directly.

#### Registry schema ŌĆö current agent shape

`RegistrySource._normalize()` produces:
`{ name, category, description, path, tags, author, version, source }`
`FileTreeSource._normalize()` adds `displayName` on top of the same fields.
Both sources already have `tags` (array) and `category` (string). NO `featured` boolean exists yet. The `tags` field is present in both the JSON schema and the FileTreeSource YAML frontmatter parsing.

#### Agent sources

- `DEFAULT_SOURCES[0]`: `RegistrySource` pointing to `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` (named `"official"`)
- `DEFAULT_SOURCES[1]`: `FileTreeSource` for `msitarzewski/agency-agents` (named `"msitarzewski/agency-agents"`)
- No static JSON committed in this repo. The official registry JSON is remote-only.
- The `RegistrySource` source name is `"official"` in code but the archived spec (`registry-bootstrap/spec.md`) says agents should display with `source: "agents-ai"`. OPEN DRIFT ŌĆö spec says `source: "agents-ai"`, code assigns `source: 'official'`. Not blocking our change but user-visible if TUI labels show source.

#### TUI pattern from `install-selection-tui`

The archived change (`openspec/changes/archive/2026-05-04-install-selection-tui/`) and the live code in `src/commands/add.js` confirm:
- Library: `inquirer` v13 (already a dependency) + `chalk` ŌĆö NO external TUI library
- `promptScope()` ŌåÆ `inquirer.prompt([{ type: 'checkbox', name: 'scope', ... }])`
- `promptTargets(tools, scope)` ŌåÆ `inquirer.prompt([{ type: 'checkbox', name: 'targets', ... }])`
- `inquirer.Separator()` for visual groupings
- Chalk colors in choice labels (bold cyan name, dim path)
- Box-drawn summary with `ŌĢŁ/Ōöé/ŌĢ░` chars via chalk
- CRITICAL: inquirer v13 does NOT have built-in fuzzy/autocomplete. Options: (a) `inquirer-autocomplete-prompt` plugin, (b) two-step input+list, (c) simple `list` prompt with all agents. Current registry size is small (~4 seeds + FileTreeSource agents) ŌĆö simple `list` is sufficient now.

#### Tests coverage

- `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö covers `RegistrySource`, `listAgents()`, `findAgent()`, `getAgentDownloadUrl()`. Source-injection via `{ sources }` param. Mock pattern: `vi.hoisted()` + `vi.mock('../src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js', ...)`.
- `tests/search.test.js` ŌĆö covers `searchAgents()` extensively (matching, edge cases, multi-source). Same mock pattern.
- No test currently covers `--tag`, `--query`, `--limit`, `--page` flags (they don't exist yet).
- No test covers TUI fuzzy-filter behavior in `list`.
- The `{ sources }` injection pattern is the established way to test registry functions without network calls. TUI path requires `vi.mock('inquirer')`.

---

### Affected Areas

- `src/commands/list.js` ŌĆö Add `--query`, `--tag`, `--limit`, `--page` flags; add TUI branch when no flags; reuse `searchAgents()` for `--query`.
- `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö `listAgents()` signature needs `{ tag, limit, page }` options; `searchAgents()` already handles query; `_normalize()` in `RegistrySource` needs `featured` field pass-through if added to registry JSON.
- `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js` ŌĆö `_normalize()` needs `featured` field if added to YAML frontmatter.
- `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö New tests for each new flag, TUI path (inquirer mock), pagination logic. Strict TDD: tests FIRST.
- Registry JSON at `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` (external repo) ŌĆö Add `category`, `tags`, and optionally `featured` fields. Must be coordinated separately.

---

### Key Design Decisions for Proposal

1. **Reuse `searchAgents()` for `--query`** ŌĆö already matches across all text fields. Zero new core logic.
2. **Tag filtering: extend `listAgents()`** ŌĆö add `{ tag }` filter: `agents.filter(a => a.tags.includes(tag))`. Simple, localized.
3. **Pagination: client-side slice** ŌĆö `agents.slice(page * limit, (page + 1) * limit)` before `groupByCategory()`. No server changes.
4. **TUI: inquirer `list` prompt** ŌĆö same pattern as add.js. Show featured first (sorted), then alphabetical. No new dependency.
5. **`featured` field** ŌĆö new schema addition. Add to `RegistrySource._normalize()` and `FileTreeSource._normalize()`. Fallback: missing field ŌåÆ `false`.

---

### Source Drift Flag (OPEN)

`openspec/specs/registry-bootstrap/spec.md` says agents from the official source should show `source: "agents-ai"`. Code in `src/core/registry.js` has `new RegistrySource({ name: 'official', ... })` which produces `source: 'official'`. This mismatch exists today and is not introduced by our change. The proposal must decide: fix in this change (rename source to `"agents-ai"`) or leave it.

---

### Approaches

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|----------|------|------|--------|
| 1. CLI flags only (`--query`, `--tag`, `--limit`, `--page`) | Scriptable/CI-friendly; reuses `searchAgents()`; no new dependencies; straightforward test path | No TUI; `featured` field still needs external registry change; pagination on small dataset may feel premature | Low |
| 2. TUI-only (simple `list` prompt, no live fuzzy) | Same inquirer pattern as add.js; no new dependencies; shows all agents interactively | Not scriptable; no filtering flags; `featured` still needs new schema field | Medium |
| 3. Full strategy (flags + TUI branch + `featured` field) ŌĆö RECOMMENDED | Matches agreed strategy exactly; progressive: flags for CI, TUI for humans; `featured` sorts curated agents first | Wider scope; three layers; `featured` requires external registry JSON change; no live fuzzy without new dependency | Medium-High |
| 4. Full strategy + `inquirer-autocomplete-prompt` | Adds true live fuzzy to TUI | Adds dependency; unknown test story for plugin; risk on Windows terminal | High |

**Recommended:** Approach 3. Matches agreed decision. Use simple inquirer `list` prompt for TUI ŌĆö good enough now. Upgrade to fuzzy later.

---

### Risks

- **External registry JSON change:** `featured` field and richer `tags` data must exist in the remote registry before CLI ships or must degrade gracefully (missing `featured` ŌåÆ `false`).
- **No live fuzzy without new dependency:** inquirer v13 cannot do real-time narrowing natively. Manage expectations or add `inquirer-autocomplete-prompt` consciously.
- **`source: 'official'` vs `'agents-ai'` drift:** If TUI displays source labels, users will see `[official]` not `[agents-ai]`. Decide in proposal.
- **Strict TDD Mode:** Every new flag and TUI branch needs a test FIRST. The TUI path is harder to test (requires full inquirer mock). Budget extra time.
- **Pagination on small registry:** May feel like premature UI noise with current ~4 seeds. Consider making `--limit`/`--page` hidden/advanced flags initially.

---

### Ready for Proposal

Yes. All three strategy layers are fully understood from the codebase. The proposal can define exact function signatures, new CLI flag names, the `featured` field contract, TUI flow, and test requirements.

observations.title: openspec/config.yaml strict_tdd discrepancy
observations.content:
**What**: `openspec/config.yaml` has `strict_tdd: false` but the orchestrator session context says Strict TDD Mode is ACTIVE.
**Why**: The config.yaml was likely written before TDD was enabled at session level. The session-level setting overrides config.
**Where**: `openspec/config.yaml` line 10
**Learned**: Do not trust openspec/config.yaml `strict_tdd` field alone ŌĆö the orchestrator's session context (from sdd-init engram observation) is the authoritative source for strict_tdd status.

observations.title: sdd/list-discovery-scaling/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: list-discovery-scaling

## Intent
Scale the `list` command for a growing registry by adding query/tag filters, client-side pagination, an inquirer TUI browse mode for empty invocations, and a `featured` field so curated agents surface first. Pragmatic client-side step before any server-side search is justified.

## Scope

### In Scope
- New flags: `--query`, `--tag`, `--limit`, `--page` (defaults `--limit 20`, `--page 1`; both advertised in `--help`).
- `--query` reuses `searchAgents()`; `--tag` filters via `tags.includes()`.
- Client-side pagination: `slice((page-1)*limit, page*limit)`.
- TUI mode: when no flags AND `process.stdout.isTTY`, open inquirer v13 `list` prompt; selecting prints details (no install).
- TUI ordering: `featured: true` first (alphabetical), then remaining alphabetical.
- New optional `featured: boolean` on agent schema; pass-through in `RegistrySource._normalize` and `FileTreeSource._normalize`; missing ŌåÆ `false`.
- Coordinate (non-blocking) with external `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` to populate `featured`.

### Out of Scope
- Live fuzzy filter inside TUI (deferred; would need `inquirer-autocomplete-prompt`).
- Server-side search / indexing.
- Fixing `source: 'official'` vs `'agents-ai'` drift (orthogonal ŌĆö separate cleanup change).
- Changes to `add` or `search` commands.
- Any new runtime dependency.

## Capabilities

### New
- None.

### Modified
- `list-command`: new flags, TUI branch, featured-aware ordering. `--category` unchanged.
- `registry-fetch`: agent schema gains optional `featured: boolean`, default `false`.
- `file-tree-source`: `_normalize` passes through optional `featured` from frontmatter, default `false`.

## Approach
1. Extend `listAgents({ category, tag, limit, page })` in `src/core/registry.js`. Order: category filter ŌåÆ tag filter ŌåÆ pagination ŌåÆ group by category.
2. Add `featured ?? false` pass-through in both `_normalize()` methods.
3. `src/commands/list.js`:
   - Parse new flags via commander.
   - `--query` ŌåÆ `searchAgents(query)` then apply tag/limit/page on flat list.
   - No flags + TTY ŌåÆ `runBrowseTUI(agents)` with featured-first inquirer `list` prompt and `inquirer.Separator()`.
   - Otherwise text rendering after filter+paginate.
4. Strict TDD (vitest run): tests FIRST for each flag, pagination math, `featured` ordering, and TUI branch via `vi.mock('inquirer')`. Use existing `{ sources }` injection and `vi.hoisted()` pattern.

## Affected Areas
- `src/commands/list.js` ŌĆö flags, TUI branch, ordering.
- `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö `listAgents` signature; `RegistrySource._normalize` featured pass-through.
- `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js` ŌĆö `_normalize` featured pass-through.
- `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö new specs.
- External registry JSON ŌĆö coordinated, non-blocking.

## Risks
| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| External registry lacks `featured` at ship | High | `?? false` default; CLI works regardless |
| Pagination premature on small dataset | Med | Defaults only kick in if user passes flags; default list still shows up to 20 |
| TUI hard to test under strict TDD | Med | `vi.mock('inquirer')` like `tests/add.test.js`; keep TUI thin |
| Users expect live fuzzy | Low-Med | Document filters live behind CLI flags; revisit fuzzy later |
| `source: 'official'` drift visible in TUI | Low | Don't display source label in TUI choices this iteration |

## Rollback
Revert commits touching `src/commands/list.js`, `src/core/registry.js`, `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js`, `tests/list.test.js`. No migrations. `featured` is additive/optional ŌĆö external JSON updates harmless if left in place.

## Dependencies
- inquirer v13, chalk, commander v13 (all existing).
- Non-blocking coordination with `ruben-salas20/agents-ai-registry` for `featured` data.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `list --query foo` substring-matches name/displayName/category/description/tags.
- [ ] `list --tag bar` filters by tag membership.
- [ ] `list --limit 5 --page 2` returns items 6ŌĆō10 in stable order.
- [ ] `list` on TTY with no flags opens inquirer prompt; featured first.
- [ ] Missing `featured` treated as `false`, no crash.
- [ ] All new behavior covered by vitest tests written before implementation.
- [ ] No new runtime dependency.
- [ ] `--help` documents the four new flags.

observations.title: sdd/list-discovery-scaling/spec
observations.content:
# Delta Spec: list-discovery-scaling

---

## Domain: list-command

### MODIFIED Requirements

#### Requirement: List available agents

The system MUST provide a `list` command that displays available agents using fetched registry data. When invoked with no flags in a TTY context, the command MUST open an interactive TUI browse prompt. When invoked with no flags in a non-TTY context (piped/CI), the command MUST fall back to paginated text output. When invoked with any flag, the command MUST apply the corresponding filter or pagination and render text output.
(Previously: always rendered text output regardless of terminal context.)

##### Scenario: Render list output ŌĆö non-TTY or with flags

- GIVEN registry retrieval returns one or more agents
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list` in a non-TTY context, OR with any flag
- THEN the CLI prints agents in readable grouped text output
- AND the command exits successfully

##### Scenario: TUI browse mode ŌĆö no flags, TTY

- GIVEN registry data is available and stdout is a TTY
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list` with no flags
- THEN an inquirer `list` prompt opens showing all agents
- AND featured agents appear first (alphabetical within group), then non-featured alphabetical
- AND selecting an agent prints that agent's details to stdout
- AND the command exits successfully after selection

##### Scenario: TUI falls back to text in non-TTY

- GIVEN stdout is not a TTY (e.g. piped output or CI)
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list` with no flags
- THEN the CLI renders paginated text output
- AND no interactive prompt is opened

---

#### Requirement: Category filtering

The system SHOULD support category filtering for `list` using a `--category` option. (Unchanged behavior.)

##### Scenario: Filter by category

- GIVEN registry data includes multiple categories
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --category engineering`
- THEN only agents in `engineering` are shown

##### Scenario: No matches for category

- GIVEN no agents match the requested category
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --category <value>`
- THEN the CLI reports that no matching agents were found
- AND the command exits without an unhandled error

---

### ADDED Requirements

#### Requirement: Query flag ŌĆö substring search

The system MUST support a `--query <q>` flag that filters agents using case-insensitive substring matching across `name`, `displayName`, `category`, `description`, and `tags`.

##### Scenario: Query matches multiple agents

- GIVEN registry data contains agents with varied names and descriptions
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --query foo`
- THEN only agents whose name, displayName, category, description, or tags contain "foo" (case-insensitive) are shown

##### Scenario: Query yields no matches

- GIVEN no agent matches the query string
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --query <value>`
- THEN the CLI reports no matching agents were found
- AND exits successfully

---

#### Requirement: Tag flag ŌĆö exact tag membership filter

The system MUST support a `--tag <name>` flag that filters agents whose `tags` array contains the given value (case-insensitive).

##### Scenario: Tag filter matches agents

- GIVEN agents with `tags: ["backend", "api"]` and others without that tag
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --tag backend`
- THEN only agents whose tags include "backend" (case-insensitive) are shown

##### Scenario: Tag filter yields no matches

- GIVEN no agent carries the requested tag
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --tag <value>`
- THEN the CLI reports no matching agents and exits successfully

---

#### Requirement: Client-side pagination with --limit and --page

The system MUST support `--limit <n>` (default 20) and `--page <n>` (default 1) flags. Pagination MUST be applied client-side AFTER all filters. The output MUST display the current page number and total agent count after filtering.

##### Scenario: Paginated result within range

- GIVEN 15 filtered agents exist
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --limit 5 --page 2`
- THEN agents 6ŌĆō10 of the filtered set are shown in stable order
- AND the output indicates page 2 and total count 15

##### Scenario: Page beyond available data

- GIVEN fewer than (page ŌłÆ 1) ├Ś limit agents exist after filtering
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --limit 5 --page 99`
- THEN the CLI shows an empty result (or reports no agents on this page)
- AND exits successfully without error

##### Scenario: Default pagination applies when no flags given

- GIVEN more than 20 agents in the registry
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list` in non-TTY mode with no flags
- THEN at most 20 agents are shown
- AND pagination info (page 1, total) is displayed

---

#### Requirement: --help documents all new flags

The system MUST include `--query`, `--tag`, `--limit`, and `--page` in the `--help` output of the `list` command.

##### Scenario: Help output includes new flags

- GIVEN the CLI is built with the new flags registered
- WHEN the user runs `agents-ai list --help`
- THEN the output lists `--query`, `--tag`, `--limit`, and `--page` with descriptions and defaults

---

#### Requirement: Featured-aware ordering in TUI and text output

When displaying agents, the system MUST sort agents with `featured: true` before non-featured agents. Within each group, ordering MUST be alphabetical by `displayName` or `name`. A visual separator MUST appear between featured and non-featured groups in TUI mode.

##### Scenario: Featured agents appear first

- GIVEN a mix of featured and non-featured agents
- WHEN the agent list is rendered (TUI or text)
- THEN featured agents appear before non-featured agents
- AND each group is sorted alphabetically

##### Scenario: No featured agents ŌĆö no separator

- GIVEN no agents have `featured: true`
- WHEN the agent list is rendered
- THEN all agents appear in alphabetical order with no featured-section separator

---

## Domain: registry-fetch

### MODIFIED Requirements

#### Requirement: Merged normalized listing

The system SHALL return a single merged collection suitable for command rendering. Each normalized agent record MUST include the `featured` field; when the source data omits it, the field SHALL default to `false`.
(Previously: no `featured` field in normalized records.)

##### Scenario: Merge entries from multiple sources

- GIVEN multiple sources return valid agent records
- WHEN retrieval completes
- THEN the system returns one unified list
- AND each record retains source attribution for display

##### Scenario: Agent record missing `featured` field

- GIVEN a source returns an agent entry without a `featured` key
- WHEN the record is normalized
- THEN `featured` is set to `false` on the normalized record
- AND no error or crash occurs

##### Scenario: Agent record has `featured: true`

- GIVEN a source returns an agent entry with `featured: true`
- WHEN the record is normalized
- THEN `featured` is `true` on the normalized record

---

## Domain: file-tree-source

### MODIFIED Requirements

#### Requirement: Parse frontmatter into normalized agent records

The adapter MUST parse YAML frontmatter from each discovered markdown file and SHALL emit normalized records containing canonical slug, display fields, source attribution, and a `featured` field. When frontmatter omits `featured`, the adapter SHALL default it to `false`.
(Previously: no `featured` field passed through from frontmatter.)

##### Scenario: Valid frontmatter file

- GIVEN a markdown file with valid YAML frontmatter for an agent
- WHEN the adapter parses the file
- THEN one normalized agent record is returned

##### Scenario: Unparseable frontmatter file

- GIVEN a markdown file with invalid or missing required frontmatter
- WHEN the adapter parses the file
- THEN that file is skipped
- AND a warning is surfaced without aborting the full list

##### Scenario: Frontmatter includes `featured: true`

- GIVEN a markdown file whose frontmatter contains `featured: true`
- WHEN the adapter normalizes the record
- THEN `featured` is `true` in the emitted record

##### Scenario: Frontmatter omits `featured`

- GIVEN a markdown file whose frontmatter does not include `featured`
- WHEN the adapter normalizes the record
- THEN `featured` defaults to `false` in the emitted record
- AND no crash or warning is emitted for the missing field

observations.title: sdd/list-discovery-scaling/design
observations.content:
# Design: list-discovery-scaling

**Project:** agents-ai | **Date:** 2026-05-07 | **Strict TDD:** ACTIVE

## 1. Architecture Approach
Layered, additive change in existing `bin ŌåÆ commands ŌåÆ core ŌåÆ utils`.
- `core/registry.js` extended; new pure helpers `paginate`, `sortFeaturedFirst` colocated with `groupByCategory`.
- `commands/list.js` becomes flag parser + mode dispatcher.
- TUI extracted to new `src/commands/list/tui.js` for testability.
- Sources gain a single `featured` pass-through in `_normalize()`.
- No new runtime dependency.

## 2. Function Signatures

### listAgents(opts)
`listAgents({ category, tag, query, limit, page, sources })` returns `Map<category, Agent[]>`.

DECISION: `listAgents` calls `searchAgents` internally when `query` is set. `searchAgents` stays exported and unchanged. Keeps `list.js` thin and reuses existing matching pipeline.

Internal pipeline order:
1. query? ŌåÆ `searchAgents(query, {sources})` flatten; else `fetchAndMerge(sources)`.
2. category filter (exact, unchanged).
3. tag filter ŌĆö case-insensitive equality on tag string.
4. `sortFeaturedFirst` ŌĆö stable partition.
5. `paginate` ONLY if limit or page was passed by caller (defaults NOT auto-applied here).
6. `groupByCategory`.

### paginate(agents, {limit=20, page=1})
Private in `src/core/registry.js`, exposed via `__testExports`. 1-based; out-of-range ŌåÆ `[]`; defensive normalization (limitŌēź1, pageŌēź1).

### sortFeaturedFirst(agents)
Stable partition `featured===true` first, then rest. Preserves incoming order. Does NOT alphabetize (TUI concern).

## 3. featured Field Contract
- Strict boolean. Only `=== true` qualifies. Anything else ŌåÆ `false`.
- RegistrySource `_normalize`: `featured: raw.featured === true`.
- FileTreeSource `_normalize`: `featured: frontmatter.featured === true`.
- Missing ŌåÆ `false`. Truthy non-boolean ŌåÆ `false` (no coercion, fails loud-but-safe).
- Now part of canonical agent shape returned everywhere.

## 4. TUI Branch

### Mode precedence
- Any flag (category|tag|query|limit|page) ŌåÆ TEXT.
- No flags + `process.stdout.isTTY` ŌåÆ TUI.
- No flags + non-TTY ŌåÆ TEXT with auto defaults `limit=20, page=1` (only place defaults auto-apply).

### TUI flow (runBrowseTUI)
1. Receives flat merged agent array (no extra fetch).
2. `sortFeaturedFirst` then alphabetical by displayName||name within each partition.
3. Inquirer choices: `Separator('Ōśģ Featured')` (if any), featured items, `Separator('All agents')`, rest.
4. Choice value = agent object itself; choice name = `cyan(displayName) dim(category/slug)`. No `[source]` label.
5. `inquirer.prompt({type:'list', pageSize:15})`.
6. After pick: render compact card + install hint `agents-ai add {slug}`. NO install ŌĆö that's `add`'s job.

### Detail card
Compact custom card (NOT full markdown ŌĆö would require extra fetch). Box-draw style mirroring `add.js`. Fields: displayName, slug, version, description, tags, source, install hint.

### File location
DECISION: extract to `src/commands/list/tui.js`. Public API: `runBrowseTUI(agents, {promptFn})`, `buildBrowseChoices(agents)`, `renderAgentCard(agent)`. `promptFn` injectable; `vi.mock('inquirer')` is canonical test path.

## 5. Test Plan (Strict TDD, vitest run)

### Per flag (tests/list.test.js)
- `--query`: matches `searchAgents` output; respects dedupe; empty/whitespace ŌåÆ empty Map.
- `--tag`: case-insensitive membership; combine with `--category` (intersection).
- `featured` ordering: featured before non-featured; stable when none featured; truthy non-bool treated as false.
- Source pass-through: both `_normalize` produce correct `featured` boolean.

### TUI (new tests/list-tui.test.js)
`vi.mock('inquirer')` with mocked prompt + Separator constructor.
1. `buildBrowseChoices` ŌĆö featured first, alphabetical inside, separators correct, value=agent.
2. `runBrowseTUI` ŌĆö calls prompt once with `list` type; selection returns cleanly; no install path.
3. `renderAgentCard` ŌĆö pure string assertions.
4. Mode dispatch: TTY toggled; flagŌåÆtext path, no-flags+TTYŌåÆTUI, no-flags+non-TTYŌåÆtext with defaults.

### Pagination matrix (paginate + listAgents end-to-end)
23 agents/limit10/page1 ŌåÆ 0..9; page2 ŌåÆ 10..19; page3 ŌåÆ 20..22; page4 ŌåÆ []; 5 agents/limit20/page1 ŌåÆ all 5; limitŌēż0 normalized to 1; pageŌēż0 normalized to 1.

### Backward compat
Existing `listAgents({category})` and `listAgents()` cases unchanged. New: every agent now has `featured: false` by default.

## 6. Migration / Backward Compat
- `list -c <name>` unchanged.
- `search` command + `searchAgents()` unchanged (internal reuse only).
- `findAgent`, `getAgentDownloadUrl` untouched.
- `featured` additive; missing ŌåÆ false; current behavior preserved when no agent featured.
- Source drift (`official` vs `agents-ai`) explicitly out of scope.
- No data migration. No flag removal.

## 7. ADRs
- ADR-1: listAgents delegates to searchAgents internally (rejected: duplicate logic, push to commands).
- ADR-2: Auto-pagination only on non-TTY no-flag path (rejected: always-default would silently truncate today's output).
- ADR-3: Extract TUI to `src/commands/list/tui.js` (rejected: keep inline ŌåÆ hurts testability).
- ADR-4: featured strict boolean, no truthy coercion (rejected: Boolean() coercion ŌåÆ typo promotions).
- ADR-5: Featured-first in core; alphabetical-inside in TUI (rejected: alphabetical core ŌåÆ text-mode regression).
- ADR-6: TUI selection prints card only, never installs (rejected: auto-invoke add ŌåÆ scope creep).

## 8. 400-Line Review Budget Forecast
Estimated ~430ŌĆō490 changed lines. ABOVE 400. Delivery `auto-chain` mandates chained PRs.

### PR 1 ŌĆö Schema + featured pass-through (~50 lines)
Both `_normalize` add `featured`. Tests: `_normalize` units. Trivially mergeable, unblocks 2 & 3.

### PR 2 ŌĆö Core flags + helpers (~210 lines)
listAgents opts + searchAgents delegation; paginate; sortFeaturedFirst; flag wiring in list.js text path; non-TTY default pagination. Tests: per-flag, pagination matrix, featured ordering, backward compat. Depends on PR 1.

### PR 3 ŌĆö TUI branch (~210 lines)
`src/commands/list/tui.js` new module; TTY dispatch in list.js; `tests/list-tui.test.js` with inquirer mock. Depends on PR 2.

Stacked chain (not parallel).

## 9. Affected Areas
- `src/commands/list.js` ŌĆö flags, dispatch, non-TTY default pagination.
- `src/commands/list/tui.js` ŌĆö NEW.
- `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö listAgents opts, paginate, sortFeaturedFirst, _normalize, __testExports.
- `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js` ŌĆö _normalize featured.
- `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö extended.
- `tests/list-tui.test.js` ŌĆö NEW.

## 10. Risks
- Stacked PR review fatigue (MED) ŌĆö auto-chain mandates it; PR 1 trivial to unblock.
- Source drift visible in card (LOW) ŌĆö out of scope.
- Default `limit=20` on non-TTY surprising piped consumers (LOW) ŌĆö documented in --help.
- `featured` not yet in remote registry at ship (LOW) ŌĆö `?? false` default; coordination non-blocking.
- Inquirer v13 mock brittleness (LOW) ŌĆö already proven in tests/add.test.js.

## Spec dependency
sdd-tasks should run AFTER sdd-spec also exists for this change.

observations.title: sdd/list-discovery-scaling/tasks
observations.content:
# Tasks: list-discovery-scaling

## Review Workload Forecast

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Estimated changed lines | 430ŌĆō490 |
| 400-line budget risk | High |
| Chained PRs recommended | Yes |
| Suggested split | PR1 ŌåÆ PR2 ŌåÆ PR3 (stacked-to-main) |
| Delivery strategy | auto-chain |
| Chain strategy | stacked-to-main |

Decision needed before apply: No
Chained PRs recommended: Yes
Chain strategy: stacked-to-main
400-line budget risk: High

### Suggested Work Units

| Unit | Goal | Likely PR | Notes |
|------|------|-----------|-------|
| 1 | `featured` field in both normalizers | PR1 | Base = main; ~50 LOC; unblocks PR2+PR3 |
| 2 | Core helpers + flag expansion + text wiring | PR2 | Base = PR1 branch; ~210 LOC; depends on PR1 |
| 3 | TUI module + TTY dispatch | PR3 | Base = PR2 branch; ~210 LOC; depends on PR2 |

---

## PR1 ŌĆö Schema: featured pass-through (~50 LOC) Ō£ģ COMPLETE

**Goal**: Both source normalizers emit a strict-boolean `featured` field. Trivially mergeable; unblocks PR2 and PR3.

- [x] 1.1 **[RED]** `tests/unit/sources/FileTreeSource.test.js` ŌĆö add failing tests for `_normalize` emitting `featured: true` when frontmatter contains `featured: true`, `featured: false` when omitted, and `featured: false` when frontmatter has truthy non-boolean (e.g. `"yes"`).
- [x] 1.2 **[GREEN]** `src/core/sources/FileTreeSource.js` ŌĆö in `_normalize()`, add `featured: frontmatter.featured === true` to the returned record. No other change.
- [x] 1.3 **[RED]** `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö add failing tests for `src/core/registry.js` `_normalize` (RegistrySource path): `featured: true` preserved, missing `featured` ŌåÆ `false`, truthy non-boolean ŌåÆ `false`.
- [x] 1.4 **[GREEN]** `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö in `_normalize()` (RegistrySource), add `featured: raw.featured === true` to the normalized record.
- [x] 1.5 **[VERIFY]** Run `vitest run` ŌĆö all new tests GREEN, no regressions. `featured` now part of canonical agent shape returned by both sources.

---

## PR2 ŌĆö Core flags + helpers (~210 LOC) Ō£ģ COMPLETE

**Goal**: `listAgents` accepts `{ tag, query, limit, page }` opts; `paginate` and `sortFeaturedFirst` helpers ship; `list.js` wires new flags for text-output path; non-TTY no-flag path applies default pagination.

Depends on PR1 (canonical `featured` field in agent shape).

- [x] 2.0 **[RED+GREEN]** displayName pass-through ŌĆö `RegistrySource._normalize()` now emits `displayName`. +1 task from PR1 verify suggestion S1. 3 new tests.
- [x] 2.1 **[RED]** `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö `sortFeaturedFirst` 6 tests (6.3aŌĆōf).
- [x] 2.2 **[GREEN]** `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö `sortFeaturedFirst(agents)` + `__testExports`.
- [x] 2.3 **[RED]** `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö `paginate` 8 tests (6.4aŌĆōh).
- [x] 2.4 **[GREEN]** `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö `paginate(agents, {limit=20, page=1})`.
- [x] 2.5 **[RED]** `tests/list.test.js` ŌĆö `listAgents` opts expansion 8 tests (6.2gŌĆōn).
- [x] 2.6 **[GREEN]** `src/core/registry.js` ŌĆö `listAgents({ category, tag, query, limit, page, sources })` expanded.
- [x] 2.7 **[RED]** `tests/list-flags.test.js` (NEW FILE) ŌĆö 6 flag-wiring tests.
- [x] 2.8 **[GREEN]** `src/commands/list.js` ŌĆö 4 new options + default pagination wiring.
- [x] 2.9 **[VERIFY]** `vitest run` ŌĆö 269/269 passing. 31 new tests. 0 regressions.

---

## PR3 ŌĆö TUI extraction + TTY dispatch (~210 LOC) Ō£ģ COMPLETE

**Goal**: `src/commands/list/tui.js` new module with injectable `promptFn`; `list.js` dispatches to TUI when no flags + TTY, falls back to text in non-TTY.

Depends on PR2 (sorted agents, helpers available).

- [x] 3.1 **[RED]** `tests/list-tui.test.js` (NEW) ŌĆö `buildBrowseChoices`: featured first; alphabetical by `displayName||name` within each partition; Inquirer `Separator` appears before featured group (if any) and before non-featured group; choice `value` === agent object; choice `name` includes cyan displayName + dim category/slug.
- [x] 3.2 **[GREEN]** `src/commands/list/tui.js` (NEW) ŌĆö implement `buildBrowseChoices(agents)`.
- [x] 3.3 **[RED]** `tests/list-tui.test.js` ŌĆö `renderAgentCard(agent)`: pure string assertions for displayName, slug, version, description, tags, source, and install hint `agents-ai add {slug}`.
- [x] 3.4 **[GREEN]** `src/commands/list/tui.js` ŌĆö implement `renderAgentCard(agent)`.
- [x] 3.5 **[RED]** `tests/list-tui.test.js` ŌĆö `runBrowseTUI(agents, {promptFn})`: injectable promptFn called once with {type:'list', pageSize:15, choices}.
- [x] 3.6 **[GREEN]** `src/commands/list/tui.js` ŌĆö implement `runBrowseTUI(agents, {promptFn = inquirer.prompt})`.
- [x] 3.7 **[RED]** `tests/list-mode.test.js` (NEW) ŌĆö mode dispatch: no flags + isTTY=true ŌåÆ runBrowseTUI called; no flags + isTTY=false ŌåÆ text path; any flag ŌåÆ text path.
- [x] 3.8 **[GREEN]** `src/commands/list.js` ŌĆö static import + TTY guard dispatching to runBrowseTUI.
- [x] 3.9 **[VERIFY]** `vitest run` ŌĆö 300/300 passing. 31 new tests. 13 test files. 0 regressions.

---

## Notes

- Strict TDD active: every GREEN step is preceded by its RED step in the same PR.
- `__testExports` pattern follows existing `registry.js` convention for private helpers.
- `vi.mock('inquirer')` is the canonical path for TUI tests (proven in `tests/add.test.js`).
- `src/commands/list/tui.js` requires creating the `src/commands/list/` directory first.
- `paginate` and `sortFeaturedFirst` are private to `registry.js`; exposed only via `__testExports`.
- `featured` strict-boolean (`=== true`) ŌĆö no truthy coercion per ADR-4.
- PR1 complete: 238/238 tests passing, 2 production lines changed, 6 new tests added.
- PR2 complete: 269/269 tests passing, 31 new tests, new file tests/list-flags.test.js.
- PR3 complete: 300/300 tests passing, 31 new tests (23 list-tui + 8 list-mode), 2 new files.
- PR3 dispatch deviation: test expectation adjusted ŌĆö in TUI mode listAgents IS called to fetch flat agents.
- PR3 static import used (not dynamic) to avoid Windows ESM pathToFileURL issue.
- PR2 note: test 6.2f updated ŌĆö now checks flag names (not count=1).
- PR2 note: fileTreeAgents fixture got `featured: false` on all entries.
- PR2 key decision: flag-wiring tests isolated to list-flags.test.js due to vi.mock conflict with integration tests using real listAgents.

observations.title: SDD planning complete: list-discovery-scaling (3 stacked PRs, auto-chain)
observations.content:
**What**: Cerradas las 5 fases de planning del cambio `list-discovery-scaling` (explore, propose, spec, design, tasks). Plan resultante: 23 tareas en 3 PRs encadenados bajo `auto-chain` porque el forecast (430ŌĆō490 LOC) excede el presupuesto de 400.

**Why**: El usuario pidi├│ escalar el comando `list` (era dump completo) hacia un modelo discovery-friendly. Decisi├│n cacheada: client-side pragmatic (paginaci├│n + TUI fuzzy + categor├Łas/tags) ahora; server-side endpoint cuando duela el JSON est├Ītico.

**Where**:
- Engram observations: explore #295, proposal #297, spec #298, design #299, tasks #300.
- Files: `openspec/changes/list-discovery-scaling/{explore,proposal,spec,design,tasks}.md`

**Learned**:
- `searchAgents()` ya hace match multi-field ŌĆö `list --query` lo reusa sin l├│gica nueva.
- inquirer v13 NO tiene fuzzy live nativo. Decisi├│n: simple `list` prompt para esta etapa; upgrade a `inquirer-autocomplete-prompt` queda fuera de scope.
- `featured` field es la ├║nica adici├│n al schema; fallback `?? false` para sobrevivir sin update del registry remoto.
- Drift `source: 'official'` vs `'agents-ai'` queda OPEN en otra issue (orthogonal).
- Plan de PRs: PR1 schema (~50 LOC) ŌåÆ PR2 core+flags (~210 LOC) ŌåÆ PR3 TUI (~210 LOC). PR2 depende impl├Łcitamente de PR1; PR3 depende de PR2. Stacked-to-main.

## TDD Cycle Evidence (PR3)

| Task | RED tests | GREEN | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|
| 3.1+3.2 buildBrowseChoices | 8 failures (module not found) | Ō£ģ tui.js created | RED = import error = all failing |
| 3.3+3.4 renderAgentCard | Included in same RED file | Ō£ģ Implemented in same GREEN step | Single file, single RED phase |
| 3.5+3.6 runBrowseTUI | Included in same RED file | Ō£ģ Implemented in same GREEN step | 23/23 list-tui.test.js passing |

| 3.8 list.js TTY guard | Written after RED confirmed | Ō£ģ 8/8 list-mode passing | Static import chosen |
| 3.9 VERIFY | ŌĆö | Ō£ģ 300/300 | Full suite clean |

---

## Key Decisions Made in PR3

- **Test file isolation**: dispatch tests moved to `tests/list-mode.test.js` (not `tests/list.test.js`) for the same vi.mock isolation reason as PR2's list-flags.test.js.
- **Static import** for tui.js in list.js: avoids Windows ESM dynamic import pathToFileURL complexity. Design recommended this.
- **TUI mode fetches via listAgents**: TTY path calls `listAgents({})` to get grouped map, then flattens it for TUI. Test 3.7a adjusted to verify flat array passed to runBrowseTUI rather than asserting listAgents not called.
- **Alphabetical sort inside TUI**: done within tui.js using `compareByDisplayName()` (displayName || name). Design ADR-5: featured-first in core; alphabetical-inside in TUI.
- **No install path in TUI**: card displays install hint string only. ADR-6 maintained.
- **isTTY restoration**: afterEach restores `process.stdout.isTTY` to originalIsTTY to prevent test pollution across files.

- Total tests: 300 (was 232 before this change)

- Zero regressions across all batches

observations.title: vi.mock isolation ŌĆö separate test file for command flag wiring
observations.content:
**What**: When a test file mixes integration tests (using real implementations) and unit tests that need vi.mock on the same module, the mock bleeds across all tests in the file and breaks the integration tests.

**Why**: vitest hoists vi.mock() calls to the top of the module. If list.test.js mocks registry.js AND uses listAgents directly (integration tests), ALL uses of registry.js in that file get the mock ŌĆö including the integration tests that relied on the real implementation.

**Where**: `tests/list-flags.test.js` (NEW) ŌĆö isolated from `tests/list.test.js` to avoid this conflict. list.test.js tests real listAgents behavior via mocked FileTreeSource. list-flags.test.js tests CLI flag wiring via mocked listAgents.

**Learned**: The rule is: one test file, one mock boundary. If you need to mock module A to test B, and you also need the real module A for integration tests ŌĆö put the unit tests in a separate file. This is why the project already has separate files (add.test.js vs list.test.js vs search.test.js).

observations.title: Fixed empty-list edge case in runBrowseTUI
observations.content:
**What**: `runBrowseTUI` ahora hace early-return con mensaje "No agents available." cuando la lista de agentes est├Ī vac├Ła. Antes llamaba a `inquirer.prompt({ type: 'list', choices: [] })` lo que pod├Ła colgar o tirar excepci├│n de inquirer en producci├│n.

**Why**: PR3 verify (#306) flagge├│ como WARNING que el c├│digo diverg├Ła del spec ("no prompt cuando la lista est├Ī vac├Ła"). El test 3.5d original validaba el comportamiento incorrecto (que el prompt se llame igual). Cambiado a TDD: test 3.5d ahora exige `promptFn` no llamado + mensaje al usuario.

**Where**: 
- `src/commands/list/tui.js:167` ŌĆö early return con `chalk.yellow('No agents available.')`
- `tests/list-tui.test.js:294` ŌĆö test 3.5d reescrito

**Learned**: El test original hab├Ła sido escrito para reflejar la implementaci├│n, no el spec ŌĆö un anti-pattern de TDD donde el test confirma lo que el c├│digo hace en vez de lo que debe hacer. Verify lo detect├│ comparando contra spec. Vale la pena leer el spec y no solo correr los tests durante verify.
